stores.name: 1-800 Contacts
stores.intro:
1-800 Contacts is the largest direct-to-consumer retailer of contact lenses in the
United States, shipping name-brand lenses from Acuvue, Bausch + Lomb, Alcon, and
CooperVision straight to customers who upload a valid prescription. It also runs a
telehealth-style online eye exam and prescription renewal service in some states.
There is no 1-800 Contacts co-brand credit card, so the purchase simply codes as
online shopping. A flat-rate cashback card or one with an online shopping bonus
category is the most reliable way to earn on orders, and FSA or HSA cards are
commonly accepted since lenses are an eligible medical expense.

stores.name: 1-800-Flowers
stores.intro: 1-800-Flowers delivers bouquets, plants, and gift baskets nationwide, mostly via its website and phone orders. These purchases code as online shopping rather than as a florist-specific bonus, so a flat-rate card or one rewarding online retail earns the most. Watch for occasional card-linked offers around holidays like Valentine's Day and Mother's Day.

stores.name: 1-800-PACK-RAT
stores.intro:
1-800-PACK-RAT delivers portable storage containers to a customer's driveway for
do-it-yourself packing, then either picks the unit up for offsite storage or hauls
it to a new address, competing with PODS and U-Haul in the moving and storage space.
It is owned by U-Haul's parent company but operates under its own brand and pricing.
There is no PACK-RAT credit card, and orders typically code as general online
shopping or a moving service rather than a dedicated storage category, so a flat-rate
cashback card or one with a broad online purchases bonus is the practical choice
when booking a container rental online.

affiliate.offer: up to 50% off with code SPRING50

stores.name: 1-800-PetMeds
stores.intro:
1-800-PetMeds is one of the oldest direct-to-consumer pet pharmacies in the country,
shipping prescription medications, flea and tick preventives, and general pet health
and wellness products for dogs and cats without requiring a trip to the vet or a
local pharmacy counter. It works with a customer's veterinarian to verify prescriptions
before fulfilling orders. There is no PetMeds co-brand card, so a card that bonuses
pet supplies purchases, or failing that a flat-rate cashback card, is the best way
to earn on recurring medication and wellness orders.

affiliate.offer: 40% off your first order with code NEW40

stores.name: 24 Hour Fitness
stores.intro: 24 Hour Fitness is known for round-the-clock access at many of its clubs, with tiered memberships covering different location bundles. Gym dues are almost never a bonus category, so they usually earn your card's base rate, making a card that offers a fitness or wellness credit the most practical route to extra value.

stores.name: 32 Degrees
stores.intro:
32 Degrees sells lightweight, moisture-wicking basics such as base layers, outerwear,
and everyday apparel at value prices, and it's a frequent presence in flash-sale and
daily-deal marketing. It's part of the same corporate family as several other budget
apparel brands sold online. There is no 32 Degrees credit card, so purchases simply
code as apparel or online retail. A card that bonuses online shopping or clothing
purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the straightforward way to earn on an
order.

stores.name: 5.11 Tactical
stores.intro:
5.11 Tactical makes durable pants, boots, packs, and outerwear built for law
enforcement, military, and outdoor and range use, and it has grown into a broader
workwear and everyday-carry brand sold through its own stores and website. There is
no 5.11 co-brand credit card, so a purchase simply codes as apparel or general online
shopping. A card that bonuses online purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the
most reliable way to earn rewards on gear ordered from the site.

stores.name: 7 For All Mankind
stores.intro:
7 For All Mankind is a premium denim label credited with helping popularize
high-end designer jeans in the early 2000s, and it still sells jeans, jackets,
and other apparel at a noticeable step up in price from mass-market denim brands.
It's owned by a larger apparel holding company alongside other fashion labels.
There is no 7 For All Mankind credit card, so orders code as apparel or general
online shopping. A card with an online shopping or department-store-style bonus
category, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the best way to earn on a purchase.

stores.name: 7-Eleven
stores.intro:
7-Eleven runs about 9,500 U.S. convenience stores, many with attached gas pumps. The
pumps code as gas on every card we track. In-store purchases (snacks, drinks, hot food)
typically code as convenience stores rather than as groceries or dining, which means
most 3-5% category bonuses don't apply to non-fuel purchases at 7-Eleven.

stores.name: 84 Lumber
stores.intro: 84 Lumber is one of the largest privately held suppliers of building materials in the country, geared toward contractors and serious DIY projects. Spending here typically codes as a home improvement or building supply purchase, not as a general merchant. Because lumber and materials orders can be large, a card carrying a home improvement bonus pays off, otherwise lean on your best flat-rate everyday card.

stores.name: Abercrombie & Fitch
stores.intro:
Abercrombie & Fitch is the namesake adult apparel chain of Abercrombie & Fitch Co,
which also owns Hollister and the kids brand abercrombie kids. Stores run in roughly
200 U.S. mall locations alongside a strong online business at abercrombie.com. There
is no current open co-brand credit card after the prior program ended, so shoppers
typically lean on department store category bonuses in person and online shopping
multipliers for site purchases.

stores.name: Academy Sports + Outdoors
stores.intro: Academy Sports + Outdoors is a Texas-rooted chain selling athletic gear, firearms, and outdoor equipment, and its stores tend to code as a department store. That means grocery and dining bonuses do not apply to a sporting-goods run. Online orders at academy.com usually code as online shopping, so a card rewarding online spend or a flat everyday rate is the practical choice.

stores.name: Accor
stores.intro:
Accor is the Paris-based global hotel group that operates more than 5,500 properties across
brands including Raffles, Fairmont, Sofitel, Pullman, Mövenpick, Novotel, Mercure, ibis, and
the lifestyle SO/, 25hours, and Mama Shelter labels. The ALL (Accor Live Limitless) loyalty
program is a transfer partner of Bilt at 1:3, making it one of the few ways US cardholders
earn meaningful Accor status. Accor stays code as lodging/hotels on every US card. Hotel-bonus
cards earn full rate: Chase Sapphire Reserve 4x direct, Capital One Venture X 2x base.

stores.name: Ace Hardware
stores.intro:
Ace Hardware is a cooperative of about 5,000 independently-owned hardware stores in the
U.S., smaller and more service-oriented than Home Depot or Lowe's. Ace's own Ace Rewards
loyalty program layers on top of card cashback. Notably, Ace Hardware is one of the
merchants that earns 3% on the Apple Card via Apple Pay, alongside the home-improvement
category match on general-purpose cards.

stores.name: Acer
stores.intro: Acer sells affordable laptops, Chromebooks, monitors and desktops through its website and major retailers. Direct orders generally code as online shopping or general merchandise, so a card with an online or everyday bonus is the smart pick, and routing through a card portal can pile on extra rewards.

stores.name: Acme Markets
stores.intro:
Acme Markets is the Mid-Atlantic banner of Albertsons Companies, with around 160
stores across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Delaware, Maryland, and New York. The chain
codes as a U.S. supermarket for credit cards, so a grocery rewards card (Amex Gold,
Blue Cash Preferred, Citi Custom Cash) earns its posted multiplier at checkout. Acme
participates in the Albertsons for U loyalty program and stocks a wide gift-card rack
at the service desk, which is occasionally useful for extending a grocery multiplier
into adjacent spending categories.

stores.name: Adam & Eve
stores.intro:
Adam & Eve is a long-running direct-to-consumer retailer of adult products, sold
through its own website and a chain of retail stores, with roots going back to
the 1970s as an early mail-order operation in the category. It ships discreetly
and also runs frequent promotional and clearance sales online. There is no Adam
& Eve credit card, so an order simply codes as general online shopping or retail.
A flat-rate cashback card or one that bonuses online purchases is the practical
way to earn rewards on the site.

stores.name: Adidas
stores.intro:
Adidas is the second-largest global athletic apparel brand. Adidas.com codes as
online shopping; in-store charges code as department stores. The adiClub loyalty
program issues spend-tier credits and member-exclusive product drops. Strongest
pairings are an online-shopping-bonus card for adidas.com or flat-rate cashback
for in-store.

stores.name: Adorama
stores.intro:
Adorama is a New York City photo, video, audio, and computer retailer that competes
directly with B&H Photo, operating both a Manhattan showroom and a large
e-commerce store at adorama.com. Adorama is known for its EDU pricing, used gear
marketplace, and Adorama Rewards loyalty program. Purchases typically code as
online shopping or electronics on most credit card networks.

Cards with selectable online shopping bonuses, including the Bank of America
Customized Cash and U.S. Bank Cash+, can earn 3 to 5 percent on Adorama orders, and
the Chase Freedom Flex occasionally features online shopping in its rotating
quarters. Adorama also accepts Affirm financing on larger camera bodies and lenses.

stores.name: Advance Auto Parts
stores.intro:
Advance Auto Parts is a U.S. auto parts retailer with about 4,700 domestic
stores plus the Carquest commercial parts network. In-store charges code as
auto parts; advance auto parts.com codes as online shopping. The Speed Perks
loyalty program is free and issues percent-back rewards in store credit. Pair
with flat-rate cashback or an online-shopping-bonus card for digital orders.

stores.name: Aerie
stores.intro:
Aerie is American Eagle Outfitters' intimates, loungewear, and activewear sub-brand,
with hundreds of standalone stores and a heavy presence on ae.com. The Real Rewards
credit card from Synchrony covers both American Eagle and Aerie, paying boosted points
on every purchase. On most general-purpose cards, Aerie purchases code as department
store in person and online shopping when checking out through the AE site or app.

stores.name: Aeropostale
stores.intro: Aeropostale is a teen-focused mall apparel brand selling casual basics, hoodies, and logo tees. Buying online runs through as online shopping or general retail, where a portal or rotating-category bonus can help, while in-store swipes usually fall to your flat-rate card since clothing rarely earns a standalone bonus.

stores.name: Aesop
stores.intro:
Aesop is an Australian skincare, haircare, and fragrance brand known for its
minimalist amber-glass packaging and botanically formulated products, sold
through standalone boutiques, department store counters, and its own website.
L'Oreal acquired the brand in 2023. There is no Aesop credit card, so purchases
code as general online shopping or beauty retail depending on the merchant
category assigned by the card network. A flat-rate cashback card or one that
bonuses online shopping is the straightforward way to earn on an order.

stores.name: Agent Provocateur
stores.intro:
Agent Provocateur is a British luxury lingerie and swimwear label founded in
London in the 1990s, known for high-end designs sold through its own boutiques
and website at a noticeable premium over mass-market lingerie brands. There is
no Agent Provocateur credit card, so purchases code as apparel or general online
shopping. A card that bonuses online shopping or clothing purchases, or a
flat-rate cashback card, is the best way to earn rewards on an order.

stores.name: Air Canada
stores.intro:
Air Canada is Canada's flag carrier and a Star Alliance founding member, operating hubs at
Toronto Pearson, Montreal-Trudeau, and Vancouver with an extensive transborder, transatlantic,
and transpacific network plus regional service through Air Canada Express. The Aeroplan
loyalty program is one of the strongest airline currencies in North America, with transfer
partners across Amex Membership Rewards, Capital One miles, Chase Ultimate Rewards, and Bilt.
Air Canada ticket purchases code as airlines/airfare, so airfare-bonus cards earn full rate.

stores.name: Air France / KLM
stores.intro:
Air France-KLM is the Franco-Dutch parent of Air France (hubbed at Paris-Charles de Gaulle)
and KLM Royal Dutch Airlines (hubbed at Amsterdam-Schiphol), both SkyTeam members with a
shared Flying Blue loyalty program. Flying Blue partners with Amex Membership Rewards, Chase
Ultimate Rewards, Capital One miles, Bilt, and Citi ThankYou Points as a 1:1 transfer
partner, plus periodic transfer bonuses. Air France and KLM ticket purchases code as
airlines/airfare on US cards, earning the full airfare bonus on any card with that category.

stores.name: Airalo
stores.intro:
Airalo is an eSIM marketplace that sells prepaid mobile data plans for travelers,
letting a phone connect to a local or regional carrier's network abroad without
swapping a physical SIM card. It covers plans for well over 100 countries and is
commonly used as an alternative to international roaming fees. There is no Airalo
credit card, and eSIM purchases typically code as either travel or a phone or
telecom service depending on the issuer, so a card that bonuses travel purchases,
or a flat-rate cashback card, is the reasonable choice when buying a plan.

stores.name: Airbnb
stores.intro:
Airbnb is the dominant short-term-rental marketplace, with millions of listings across
homes, apartments, cabins, and unique stays in roughly 220 countries. The wrinkle for
credit-card rewards is that Airbnb does not code as "hotels" — it codes as a generic
travel merchant. That means hotel-category bonuses (Marriott co-brand 6x, Amex Gold
hotel rate via Resy collection, Hilton Surpass 12x at hotels) generally do not trigger
on an Airbnb charge. The right cards for Airbnb are broad travel-category earners:
Sapphire Reserve and Preferred at 3x and 2x travel, Venture and Venture X at 2x flat
with no foreign transaction fees, Amex Green at 3x travel, or flat-rate 2%+ cashback
cards. Booking through the Chase or Capital One travel portals can layer additional
multipliers on top, though portal availability for Airbnb is limited.

faq.q: Does Airbnb count as a hotel for credit card rewards?
faq.a: No. Airbnb codes as a general travel merchant, not lodging or hotels. Cards that bonus on "hotels" specifically (most co-brand hotel cards, some category bonuses tied to hotel merchant codes) typically do not earn the elevated rate on Airbnb stays. Cards that bonus on "travel" broadly — Sapphire Reserve/Preferred, Venture/Venture X, Amex Green — do earn at their travel rate on Airbnb.

faq.q: What's the best credit card for Airbnb?
faq.a: For most people: Chase Sapphire Reserve at 3x travel (or Sapphire Preferred at 2x) if you want flexible points, Capital One Venture X at 2x with travel credits and no foreign transaction fees, or a flat 2%+ cashback card like Citi Double Cash if you want simplicity. Hotel co-brand cards generally don't help here.

faq.q: Can I earn hotel elite credits or points on Airbnb stays?
faq.a: No. Airbnb is not part of any hotel loyalty program — stays earn no Bonvoy, Honors, World of Hyatt, IHG One, Wyndham, or Choice points, and don't count toward elite-night requirements. The only rewards on an Airbnb stay come from your credit card.

stores.name: Alamo
stores.intro: Alamo is a major value-focused rental brand under the Enterprise Holdings family, popular with leisure travelers and airport pickups. Rentals code as car rental, a travel subset, so they earn on cards with a travel or specific rental-car bonus. Many travel cards also bundle secondary or primary rental collision coverage, which is worth checking before declining the counter waiver.

stores.name: Alaska Airlines
stores.intro:
Alaska Airlines is the West Coast's dominant carrier and runs the Mileage Plan
program, prized for its generous partner award chart and a unique stopover-on-one-way
rule. Alaska tickets code as airfare on every card we track. Alaska's co-branded
Visa offers a Companion Fare each year that's often the single biggest reason
cardholders keep it, plus a free checked bag. General travel cards usually win on
cash-equivalent earn, but the Companion Fare can outweigh that for a once-a-year
long-haul booking.

stores.name: Albertsons
stores.intro:
Albertsons is a national supermarket operator with about 2,250 stores under the
Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Jewel-Osco, Shaw's, Acme, Tom Thumb, and Randalls banners.
All banners code as groceries at the register and ship online through their respective
apps and sites. Albertsons' Just for U coupons stack with card-side rewards at checkout.

stores.name: Aldi
stores.intro:
Aldi is the discount-focused private-label grocer with about 2,400 U.S. stores and a
sharply lower price floor than full-line supermarkets. Average tickets are smaller, so
the absolute dollar value of a 3-6% grocery card is lower per visit, but the rate still
applies. Aldi codes as groceries on every card we track. Note: Aldi historically only
accepted debit and SNAP, but all U.S. stores now accept credit cards.

stores.name: ALDO
stores.intro: ALDO is a global footwear and accessories brand selling dress shoes, boots, and handbags. Shopping online codes as online shopping or general retail and may earn via a portal, but in-store visits typically fall to a flat-rate card because shoes are seldom a standalone bonus category.

stores.name: Alice + Olivia
stores.intro:
Alice + Olivia is a New York contemporary fashion label known for playful, feminine
dresses, separates, and accessories sold through its own boutiques, department
stores, and website. Founder Stacey Bendet has kept the brand independent and
design-led relative to larger fashion conglomerates. There is no Alice + Olivia
credit card, so an order codes as apparel or general online shopping. A card that
bonuses online shopping or department-store purchases, or a flat-rate cashback
card, is the practical way to earn on a purchase.

stores.name: AliExpress
stores.intro:
AliExpress is Alibaba's consumer-facing global marketplace, connecting Chinese
manufacturers and merchants with international buyers. The platform sells everything
from electronics and apparel to home goods at low price points, with shipping times
that vary widely by item. Purchases code as online shopping for credit card networks,
so an online-shopping category bonus card earns its multiplier. Some AliExpress sellers
invoice through international processors, so foreign transaction fees can apply on
cards without an FTF waiver, which is worth checking before a large order.

stores.name: Allegiant Air
stores.intro:
Allegiant Air is a Las Vegas-based ultra-low-cost carrier that connects small and mid-sized
US cities to leisure destinations like Las Vegas, Orlando-Sanford, Phoenix-Mesa, and Florida
Gulf Coast airports, flying point-to-point on a fleet of Airbus A319/A320 aircraft. Like
other ULCCs, Allegiant unbundles fares: bags, seat assignments, and printed boarding passes
are all add-ons. Allegiant purchases code as airlines/airfare on every card network. Travel
cards with airfare bonuses (Amex Gold 3x on flights booked direct, Chase Sapphire Reserve 4x,
Capital One Venture X 2x) all earn their full rate on Allegiant tickets and ancillary fees.

stores.name: Allen Edmonds
stores.intro:
Allen Edmonds is an American maker of premium men's dress shoes and leather goods, owned by
Caleres and sold direct at allenedmonds.com and in its own stores. There is no Allen Edmonds
co-brand credit card, and because its shoes are a higher-dollar purchase, a card with a
clothing or online shopping category bonus, or a flat-rate cashback card, earns the most.

stores.name: Alo Yoga
stores.intro:
Alo Yoga is a Los Angeles activewear brand built around yoga and studio-inspired
leggings, tops, and outerwear, and it has expanded into a broader lifestyle brand
with its own cafes and wellness clubs in some cities. It sells through standalone
stores, select department stores, and its own website. There is no Alo Yoga credit
card, so purchases code as apparel or general online shopping. A card that bonuses
online shopping, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the straightforward way to earn
on an order.

stores.name: Alpha Industries
stores.intro:
Alpha Industries is an American apparel maker best known for the MA-1 bomber
jacket, originally developed as a U.S. military flight jacket and now sold as a
streetwear and outerwear staple through its own stores and website. There is no
Alpha Industries credit card, so an order codes as apparel or general online
shopping. A card that bonuses online shopping or clothing purchases, or a
flat-rate cashback card, is the practical way to earn on a purchase.

stores.name: Altra
stores.intro:
Altra is a running shoe brand known for its zero-drop platform and foot-shaped
toe box, a design that has built a dedicated following among trail and road
runners looking for a more natural running gait than traditional cushioned
shoes offer. It's owned by VF Corporation alongside other outdoor and sport
brands. There is no Altra credit card, so orders from its site code as sporting
goods or general online shopping. A card that bonuses online shopping, or a
flat-rate cashback card, is the best way to earn on a purchase.

stores.name: Amana
stores.intro:
Amana is a value-tier home appliance brand covering refrigerators, ranges,
dishwashers, and laundry machines, owned by Whirlpool Corporation and positioned
as a more budget-friendly alternative to Whirlpool's flagship brands. Appliances
are sold both through Amana's own site and through major retailers like Lowe's,
Home Depot, and Best Buy. There is no Amana credit card, so a direct purchase
codes as home improvement or general online shopping. A card that bonuses home
improvement spending, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the sensible way to earn
on a new appliance.

stores.name: Amazon

also_earns.note: If selected as one of your 2 quarterly retailers (up to $1,500/quarter, then 1.5%)

stores.intro:
Amazon.com is the dominant U.S. online retailer and a category unto itself in many
credit-card rewards programs. Several cards explicitly bonus on Amazon purchases (often
quarterly, often tied to Prime membership), and Amazon.com always codes as online
shopping for cards with that as a generic bonus category. The rule of thumb: an
Amazon-specific bonus almost always beats a generic online-shopping bonus, but caps and
Prime requirements can flip the math for heavy shoppers.

stores.name: Amazon Pharmacy
stores.intro: Amazon Pharmacy fills prescriptions and ships them to your door, and it often codes as a drugstore or pharmacy purchase, so cards with a drugstore bonus can apply. Coding is not guaranteed for an Amazon-owned merchant, though, and Prime members may find a co-branded Amazon card or a flat-rate option earns just as well.

stores.name: AMC Theatres
stores.intro:
AMC Theatres is the largest U.S. movie-theater chain with about 600 domestic
locations. In-theater and online ticket purchases code as entertainment on
every card we track. The free AMC Stubs Insider program and paid AMC Stubs
A-List ($19.95-$24.99/mo for unlimited matinees) deliver the strongest
per-visit savings. Capital One Savor cards earn 4% on entertainment, and
Citi Custom Cash earns 5% if entertainment is your top monthly category.

stores.name: American Airlines
stores.intro:
American Airlines is the world's largest carrier by fleet size and the home of the
AAdvantage loyalty program. Tickets booked at aa.com or at the airport code as
airfare on every card we track. Co-branded AAdvantage cards layer extra miles plus
perks like priority boarding and a free checked bag. General travel cards (Sapphire
Reserve, Venture X) often deliver higher cash-equivalent value if you don't need
AA-specific perks. Tickets booked through OTAs still earn miles and elite credit
if your AAdvantage number is on the reservation.

stores.name: American Eagle
stores.intro:
American Eagle is a U.S. apparel retailer with about 950 stores in its main
brand plus the Aerie intimates spinoff. In-store charges code as department
stores or apparel; ae.com codes as online shopping. The Real Rewards loyalty
program issues spend-based credits redeemable on future purchases. Strongest
pairings are an online-shopping-bonus card for ae.com orders or flat-rate
cashback in-store.

stores.name: American Standard
stores.intro:
American Standard is a longstanding U.S. maker of bathroom and kitchen fixtures,
including toilets, sinks, faucets, and bathtubs, sold directly online as well as
through home improvement retailers and plumbing supply stores. The brand has been
a fixture of American plumbing since the early 1900s. There is no American
Standard credit card, so a purchase codes as home improvement or general online
shopping. A card that bonuses home improvement spending, or a flat-rate cashback
card, is the practical way to earn on fixtures ordered for a renovation.

stores.name: American Tourister
stores.intro:
American Tourister is a budget-friendly luggage and travel bag brand owned by
Samsonite, selling hardside and softside suitcases, backpacks, and carry-ons
through its own website, department stores, and travel retailers. It's positioned
as the value option under the Samsonite umbrella. There is no American Tourister
credit card, so a purchase codes as apparel or general online shopping rather
than a travel category. A card that bonuses online shopping, or a flat-rate
cashback card, is the straightforward way to earn on new luggage.

stores.name: Amerisleep
stores.intro:
Amerisleep is a direct-to-consumer memory foam mattress company that ships
compressed mattresses in a box and sells adjustable bases, pillows, and bedding
alongside its core mattress lineup, competing with brands like Nectar and Purple
in the bed-in-a-box space. There is no Amerisleep credit card, so an order codes
as furniture or general online shopping. A card that bonuses furniture or home
purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the practical way to earn on a
mattress purchase, which is often a large single transaction worth optimizing.

stores.name: Amtrak
stores.intro: Amtrak is the US national passenger rail operator, running the Northeast Corridor and long-distance routes nationwide. Tickets usually code as travel or transit and earn on cards that bonus either, so a card counting trains as travel works best. Amtrak also offers its own Guest Rewards co-brand card for riders who travel the rails frequently.

stores.name: ANA
stores.intro: ANA (All Nippon Airways) is Japan's largest airline and a Star Alliance member, so flights purchased directly code as airline travel and earn on cards that bonus airfare or general travel. Its Mileage Club is a well-regarded transfer partner for several flexible point programs, so booking award flights can stretch value far beyond a straight cash purchase.

stores.name: Anastasia Beverly Hills
stores.intro:
Anastasia Beverly Hills is a cosmetics brand that built its name on brow products,
including its widely copied brow pencils and kits, and has since expanded into a
full makeup line covering eyeshadow palettes, highlighters, and foundation. It
sells through its own website as well as major beauty retailers like Sephora and
Ulta. There is no Anastasia Beverly Hills credit card, so a direct purchase codes
as general online shopping. A card that bonuses online shopping, or a flat-rate
cashback card, is the best way to earn on an order from the brand's own site.

stores.name: Anker
stores.intro:
Anker is a consumer electronics accessory brand best known for portable chargers,
power banks, cables, and charging hardware, and it has since expanded into
headphones, smart home devices, and robot vacuums under sub-brands like Soundcore
and eufy. It sells heavily through its own website as well as Amazon and major
electronics retailers. There is no Anker credit card, so a direct purchase codes
as electronics or general online shopping. A card that bonuses electronics stores
or online shopping, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the practical way to earn on
an order.

stores.name: Ann Taylor
stores.intro: Ann Taylor focuses on tailored women's workwear and wear-to-work staples. Shopping its site codes as online shopping or general retail, where a rotating category or portal can boost earnings, but in person the charge typically falls to a flat-rate card because apparel seldom carries a bonus rate.

stores.name: Anthropologie
stores.intro:
Anthropologie is a U.S. lifestyle retailer with about 200 stores selling apparel,
home goods, and accessories, owned by URBN (also Urban Outfitters and Free People).
In-store charges code as department stores; anthropologie.com codes as online
shopping. AnthroPerks (free) and the AnthroPerks+ paid tier add free shipping and
birthday rewards. Strongest pairings are an online-shopping-bonus card or
flat-rate cashback for in-store.

stores.name: Anytime Fitness
stores.intro: Anytime Fitness is a franchised chain offering 24/7 keyfob access to thousands of locations worldwide. Membership billing rarely falls into a bonus category, so dues usually earn your card's base rate, and pairing the charge with a card that carries a fitness or wellness credit is the smartest way to recover some value.

stores.name: Apple

also_earns.note: If selected as one of your 2 quarterly retailers (up to $1,500/quarter, then 1.5%)

stores.intro:
Apple's own retail channels — apple.com, the Apple Store app, and ~270 physical Apple
Stores — code as online shopping for most cards on the .com side and as electronics
in-store. Apple purchases through Apple Pay also enable several merchant-specific
bonuses on cards that bonus Apple Pay, but those typically require the purchase to be
made via the Apple Pay button rather than chip-and-PIN. The Apple Card itself earns 3%
on Apple-branded purchases, which is the strongest non-promotional rate you'll find
here.

stores.name: Apple Music
stores.intro: Apple Music is Apple's subscription streaming service with a large catalog, lossless audio, and spatial sound. The recurring fee codes as streaming, so a card with a streaming category bonus rewards it most, otherwise it earns flat-rate. If you already buy through Apple, some cards designed around Apple purchases can layer additional value on top of the streaming bonus.

stores.name: Applebee's
stores.intro:
Applebee's is a U.S. casual-dining chain with about 1,500 domestic locations,
owned by Dine Brands. Charges code as dining on every card we track, so any
dining-bonus card applies (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%).
Applebee's runs frequent prix-fixe deals and a free-appetizer-with-rewards
loyalty program that stack on top of any card's dining bonus.

stores.name: Arby's
stores.intro:
Arby's is a U.S. fast-food chain with about 3,300 domestic locations, focused on
roast beef and deli sandwiches. Charges code as dining on every card we track,
so any dining-bonus card (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%)
applies. The Arby's Rewards app issues per-purchase points redeemable for menu
items, stackable with card bonuses.

stores.name: ARCO
stores.intro: ARCO is a West Coast value fuel brand under Marathon, famous for low prices and a historically cash- and debit-leaning model, though card acceptance has expanded. Where cards are taken, pump charges code as a gas or fuel purchase, ideal for a card with a gas bonus. Be aware some locations add a debit fee, so check before swiping a rewards credit card for fuel.

stores.name: Arhaus
stores.intro:
Arhaus is a premium home furnishings retailer based in Boston Heights, Ohio, with
around 100 large-format Showrooms and Design Studios plus arhaus.com. The brand
emphasizes hand-crafted, artisan-sourced furniture and recently expanded its outdoor
and rug categories. The Arhaus Archcharge credit card from Comenity offers
promotional financing on big purchases, and general-purpose cards typically code
Arhaus as home improvement or department store in person and online shopping for
web orders.

stores.name: Ariat
stores.intro:
Ariat makes western and English riding boots, work boots, and equestrian-inspired
apparel, built around performance footwear technology adapted from athletic shoes
and marketed to riders, ranchers, and outdoor workers. It sells through its own
website, specialty western and tack stores, and farm supply retailers. There is
no Ariat credit card, so a direct purchase codes as apparel or general online
shopping. A card that bonuses online shopping or clothing purchases, or a
flat-rate cashback card, is the practical way to earn on a purchase.

stores.name: Art.com
stores.intro:
Art.com sells framed prints, canvas art, posters, and wall decor through a large online
catalog spanning fine art reproductions, photography, and custom framing. The site is owned
by Walmart, which acquired it in 2021 to bolster its home decor assortment. There's no
Art.com co-brand credit card, so shoppers are best served by a strong online shopping
rewards card or a general flat-rate cashback card, since orders are placed and shipped
entirely through the website rather than a physical storefront.

stores.name: Ashley Furniture HomeStore
stores.intro:
Ashley Furniture HomeStore is the largest furniture retailer in the United States by
store count, selling sofas, dining sets, mattresses, and bedroom collections through
more than 1,000 corporate and licensed locations. Furniture purchases generally code
as general retail on Visa, Mastercard, and Amex networks, so they do not trigger
the home improvement bonus that covers only Home Depot and Lowe's.

Big-ticket furniture buys are a natural fit for signup bonus minimum spend on cards
like the Chase Sapphire Preferred or Amex Gold. Ashley also pushes its own Ashley
Advantage financing card with deferred-interest promotions, though paying with a
rewards card and avoiding interest usually beats the financing math.

stores.name: ASICS
stores.intro:
ASICS is a Japanese athletic footwear and apparel maker best known for its running
shoe lines like the Gel-Kayano and Nimbus, and it has a loyal following among
serious runners and gym-goers in addition to broad casual sneaker demand. The
company also owns the Onitsuka Tiger fashion sneaker label. There is no ASICS
co-brand credit card, so a purchase from asics.com codes as apparel, sporting
goods, or general online shopping depending on the card issuer's merchant coding.
A flat-rate cashback card or one that bonuses online shopping is the safest bet.

stores.name: ASOS
stores.intro:
ASOS is a UK-based online-only fashion retailer that ships globally and serves the U.S.
through asos.com, with no physical stores. Because every purchase is online, cards with
a strong online shopping or general everyday multiplier almost always win at checkout.
ASOS does not offer a U.S. branded credit card, so loyal customers rely on either the
free Premier delivery membership or rotating category bonuses from issuers like Chase
Freedom and Discover when online shopping is featured.

stores.name: Aspen Dental
stores.intro:
Aspen Dental is a network of over 1,100 independently owned dental practices across the
United States, offering general dentistry, dentures, and emergency care with same-day
appointments at many locations. Each office is run by a local dentist, though the brand
provides shared branding and back-office support across the network. There's no Aspen
Dental co-brand credit card, so for the office visit or treatment charge itself, a general
flat-rate cashback card or a card that earns bonus rewards on general or drugstore-adjacent
purchases is the more useful strategy, since dental care doesn't map cleanly to a bonus
category most issuers track.

stores.name: Astound Broadband
stores.intro:
Astound Broadband is a cable and fiber internet, TV, and phone provider that operates under
several regional brands, including RCN, Grande Communications, and Wave, serving markets
across the Northeast, Midwest, and West Coast. It's a facilities-based ISP rather than a
reseller, competing with the larger national cable operators in its footprint. There's no
Astound co-brand credit card, so the monthly bill is best paid with a card that earns bonus
rewards on internet, cable, or streaming services, or a flat-rate cashback card if your
issuer doesn't track that category.

stores.name: ASUS
stores.intro: ASUS makes laptops, motherboards, graphics cards and ROG gaming hardware sold through its online store and partners. Buying direct usually codes as online shopping or electronics retail, so a card with an online or general retail bonus earns well, while in-store purchases at other retailers typically default to a flat rate.

stores.name: At Home
stores.intro:
At Home operates over 260 big-box home decor superstores stocking patio furniture,
seasonal decor, wall art, and bedding at warehouse-style pricing. Most major credit
cards code At Home as general retail, so it sits outside the typical home improvement
bonus that covers only Lowe's and Home Depot on cards like the Wells Fargo Bilt or
Chase Freedom rotating quarters.

Online orders through athome.com can hit online shopping bonuses on cards like the
Capital One SavorOne and Bank of America Customized Cash when that category is
selected. At Home Insider Perks members also stack store coupons with credit card
cash back, which matters on big-ticket patio and Christmas decor seasons.

stores.name: AT&T
stores.intro: AT&T provides wireless service, fiber internet and TV bundles to millions of US customers. Your monthly bill earns a bonus only on cards that reward wireless or telecom spending, otherwise it lands on the flat rate, and certain cards include cell-phone protection if you charge the bill to them.

stores.name: Athleta
stores.intro:
Athleta is Gap Inc's women's performance and athleisure brand and one of the company's
fastest-growing banners, with around 270 U.S. stores plus a strong DTC channel. Most
general-purpose cards code in-store purchases as department store and athleta.gap.com
orders as online shopping. The Gap Inc Barclays cards earn elevated points across
Athleta, Old Navy, Gap, and Banana Republic, so loyal shoppers usually consolidate
spend on that program.

stores.name: Audible
stores.intro: Audible, owned by Amazon, sells audiobooks and runs a monthly credit-based membership for listeners. Because it streams and delivers digital content, the charge codes as streaming on cards that offer a streaming bonus, otherwise dropping to flat-rate. A card with a streaming category is the way to squeeze extra points from the recurring membership fee.

stores.name: Auntie Anne's
stores.intro:
Auntie Anne's is the mall and airport hand-rolled soft pretzel chain with roughly 1,800
US locations under Focus Brands. Beyond the Original and Cinnamon Sugar pretzels, the menu
includes Pretzel Dogs, Mini Pretzel Dogs, and Lemonade Mixers. Auntie Anne's transactions
code as dining/restaurants whether you order at the counter or through the My Pretzel
Perks app. That makes any dining-bonus card a fit: Amex Gold (4x Membership Rewards), Chase
Sapphire Preferred (3x Ultimate Rewards), and Capital One SavorOne (3% cash) earn full rate.

stores.name: Autonomous
stores.intro:
Autonomous is a direct-to-consumer furniture brand focused on ergonomic office setups,
known for standing desks, ergonomic chairs, and home-office accessories sold exclusively
through its own online store. The company also markets desk accessories and robotics kits
under the same umbrella. There's no Autonomous co-brand credit card, so a furniture or
home-goods bonus category card is the best fit for larger desk and chair purchases, with a
general online shopping or flat-rate cashback card as the fallback.

stores.name: AutoZone
stores.intro:
AutoZone is the largest U.S. auto parts retailer with about 6,300 domestic
stores. In-store charges typically code as auto parts (some banks classify
as gas-adjacent), and autozone.com codes as online shopping. The AutoZone
Rewards loyalty program is free and issues a $20 reward after five $20+
purchases. Pair with a flat-rate cashback card or, for online orders, an
online-shopping-bonus card.

stores.name: Aventon
stores.intro:
Aventon designs and sells electric bicycles direct to consumers, with a lineup that spans
commuter e-bikes, cargo e-bikes, and off-road models sold online and through a growing
network of local bike shop partners. The brand has built a following for pairing
mid-range pricing with app-connected features like GPS tracking and ride analytics.
There's no Aventon co-brand credit card, so a sporting goods or general online shopping
rewards card is the better play for a bike purchase, and given the price point of most
models, a card with purchase protection or an extended warranty benefit is also worth
considering.

stores.name: Avery
stores.intro:
Avery is a longtime maker of labels, binders, name badges, and printable office and craft
supplies, sold both through its own site and through office supply retailers, mass
merchants, and craft stores nationwide. The brand is part of CCL Industries and is best
known for the label templates that ship standard with most word processors. There's no
Avery co-brand credit card, so a card that earns bonus rewards on office supply store
purchases is the natural fit when buying directly from avery.com, with a flat-rate
cashback card as a solid alternative.

stores.name: Avis
stores.intro:
Avis is one of the three large U.S. car-rental brands, sister company to Budget under
Avis Budget Group. Rentals code as car rentals on every card we track. Several
premium cards confer Avis President's Club status (Amex Platinum, Capital One Venture
X) which adds a guaranteed upgrade plus free additional driver. Booking via a card's
travel portal earns the portal bonus rate but may forfeit Avis Preferred earnings
and direct-booking promo codes.

stores.name: Away
stores.intro:
Away is a direct-to-consumer luggage brand known for its hard-shell suitcases with
built-in battery chargers, along with travel bags, packing accessories, and apparel sold
through its own stores and website. The company grew out of the mid-2010s wave of
Instagram-native travel brands and has since opened brick-and-mortar shops in several
major cities. There's no Away co-brand credit card, so a general online shopping rewards
card or a card with strong travel-purchase protections, like baggage delay or purchase
coverage, is the most useful angle for a suitcase purchase.

stores.name: B&H Photo
stores.intro:
B&H Photo Video is a New York City professional photo, video, and audio retailer
with a single SuperStore in Manhattan and a major e-commerce operation at
bhphotovideo.com. The retailer is the go-to for cameras, lenses, lighting, computers,
and pro audio gear. Most credit cards treat B&H purchases as online shopping or
general electronics rather than a dedicated category.

Bank of America Customized Cash and U.S. Bank Cash+ can route B&H orders into a
selectable 5 percent online shopping bonus, while the Chase Freedom Flex sometimes
features online shopping in its rotating quarters. B&H also issues its own Payboo
card that rebates sales tax in many states, which can outweigh credit card rewards
on large camera and lens purchases.

stores.name: Babylist
stores.intro:
Babylist is an online baby registry and marketplace that lets expecting parents build a
single universal registry pulling in products from any retailer, alongside its own
Babylist Shop for gear, nursery items, and essentials. The platform has become one of the
most widely used registry tools in the U.S., expanding into health content and a Health
brand for insurance-covered breast pumps. There's no Babylist co-brand credit card, so
purchases through the Babylist Shop are best routed through a general online shopping
rewards card or a flat-rate cashback card.

stores.name: Banana Republic
stores.intro:
Banana Republic is the upscale apparel brand within Gap Inc, positioned above Gap and
Old Navy with workwear, suiting, and elevated basics across roughly 400 stores plus a
growing factory outlet network. The in-house Banana Republic Rewards Mastercard from
Barclays earns accelerated points across all Gap Inc banners, while general-purpose
cards typically code purchases as department store in-mall and online shopping for
bananarepublic.gap.com orders.

stores.name: Banana Republic Factory
stores.intro:
Banana Republic Factory is the outlet arm of Banana Republic, selling discounted apparel
and accessories both through standalone outlet stores and its own website, with a mix of
mainline overstock and outlet-specific designs. It operates as part of Gap Inc. alongside
Old Navy, Athleta, and the mainline Banana Republic brand. There's no Banana Republic
Factory co-brand credit card, since the Gap Inc. family's co-brand cards are tied to the
Gap Good Rewards program rather than the outlet banner specifically, so a select clothing
or general online shopping rewards card is the better default for purchases here.

stores.name: Barefoot Dreams
stores.intro:
Barefoot Dreams is a California loungewear brand best known for its CozyChic blanket and
cardigan line, sold through its own boutiques, website, and select department stores and
specialty retailers. The brand built its reputation on plush, machine-washable throws that
became a popular gift item well beyond its original loungewear niche. There's no Barefoot
Dreams co-brand credit card, so a select clothing or general online shopping rewards card
is the most useful category to lean on when buying directly from the brand.

affiliate.offer: up to 70% off with code DREAM15

stores.name: BarkBox
stores.intro:
BarkBox is the flagship subscription service from Bark, sending a themed monthly box
of toys, treats, and chews tailored to a dog's size. Bark also operates Super Chewer
for heavy chewers, Bright dental subscriptions, and Bark Food. As a recurring online
subscription, BarkBox charges typically code as online shopping or direct marketing
on most credit card networks.

Cards with online shopping bonuses can pay better on BarkBox renewals. Bank of
America Customized Cash and U.S. Bank Cash+ both offer selectable online shopping
categories worth 3 to 5 percent, and the Chase Freedom Flex sometimes features
online shopping in its rotating 5 percent quarter. BarkBox also frequently appears
in shopping portals like Rakuten with stacking cash back.

stores.name: Barnes & Noble
stores.intro:
Barnes & Noble is the largest bookstore chain in the United States, operating
over 600 superstores plus a major e-commerce site at barnesandnoble.com and the
Nook digital reading platform. In-store and online book purchases generally code as
bookstore or general retail on Visa, Mastercard, and Amex networks rather than
triggering a dedicated bonus category.

Cards with selectable online shopping categories, including Bank of America
Customized Cash and U.S. Bank Cash+, can earn 3 to 5 percent on barnesandnoble.com
orders. The B&N Premium Membership runs about $40 a year and includes 10 percent
off in store and free shipping online, which can stack with credit card rewards on
frequent buyers' purchases.

stores.name: Bartell Drugs
stores.intro:
Bartell Drugs is a Pacific Northwest pharmacy chain founded in 1890, with roughly 65
locations clustered in the Seattle metro and Puget Sound region. Owned by Rite Aid
since 2020, Bartell still operates under its own banner with its own pharmacy and
loyalty program. Stores code as drugstores for credit card networks, so a drugstore
category bonus card (Amex Blue Cash Preferred, Citi Custom Cash on drugstores, Chase
Freedom Flex when activated) earns its full multiplier on prescriptions and front-of-
store purchases.

stores.name: Baskin-Robbins
stores.intro:
Baskin-Robbins, the 31 Flavors ice cream chain owned by Inspire Brands (the same parent as
Dunkin'), operates roughly 2,300 US scoop shops along with a global footprint exceeding
7,000 locations. The menu spans hand-packed pints, ice cream cakes, Cappuccino Blasts, and
rotating monthly flavors. Baskin-Robbins purchases code as dining/restaurants on every US
credit card, so cards with dining bonuses earn their full multiplier: Amex Gold at 4x
Membership Rewards, Chase Sapphire Preferred at 3x, and Capital One SavorOne at 3% cash back.

stores.name: Bass Pro Shops
stores.intro: Bass Pro Shops, the fishing and hunting megastore now combined with Cabela's, often codes as a department store at its showroom-style locations, which means grocery and dining bonuses will not apply. Online orders at basspro.com typically code as online shopping. There is a co-branded Bass Pro card for fans, but a strong flat-rate or online card covers most shoppers.

stores.name: Bath & Body Works
stores.intro:
Bath & Body Works is a U.S. personal-care chain with about 1,800 domestic stores,
selling soaps, lotions, candles, and home fragrance. In-store charges code as
department stores; bathandbodyworks.com codes as online shopping. The My Bath &
Body Works Rewards program issues a free reward at sign-up plus per-spend points;
a Comenity Bank-issued co-branded credit card layers additional cashback for
heavy shoppers.

stores.name: Batteries Plus
stores.intro:
Batteries Plus is a national retail chain selling batteries, light bulbs, phone-repair
services, and key fobs through more than 700 stores and batteriesplus.com. There is no
Batteries Plus co-brand credit card, so in-store and online purchases earn on whatever card
you use. A card with a strong online shopping bonus or a reliable flat-rate cashback rate is
the simplest way to earn on both replacement batteries and one-off repairs.

stores.name: Beautyrest
stores.intro:
Beautyrest is a longstanding mattress brand under Serta Simmons Bedding, known for
pocketed-coil innerspring designs and, more recently, cooling and hybrid mattress lines
sold online and through furniture and mattress retailers nationwide. It's one of the
oldest mattress brands in the U.S. market, with roots dating back over a century. There's
no Beautyrest co-brand credit card, so a furniture store bonus category card is the
strongest fit for a mattress purchase, with a general online shopping or flat-rate
cashback card as the alternative when buying direct from beautyrest.com.

stores.name: Bed Bath & Beyond
stores.intro: Bed Bath & Beyond now lives mainly as an online home-goods brand under Beyond, Inc. after its stores closed, so purchases at bedbathandbeyond.com generally code as online shopping or general retail. Despite the home category, these orders rarely trigger a home-improvement bonus the way a hardware warehouse might. A card rewarding online spend or a flat rate is the safe bet.

stores.name: Belk
stores.intro:
Belk is a Southeast U.S. department store chain with about 290 locations across 16
states, anchored in the Carolinas. The merchandise mix is comparable to a regional
Macy's: apparel, cosmetics, accessories, and home goods, with a focus on national and
private-label brands. Belk codes as a department store, so a department-store
category bonus card earns its full rate. The Belk Rewards Mastercard (issued by
Synchrony) layers store-specific Belk Rewards points on top, with extra earn during
promotional events.

stores.name: Bellroy
stores.intro:
Bellroy is an Australian maker of slim leather wallets, bags, and phone cases, sold mostly
direct-to-consumer at bellroy.com. There is no Bellroy co-brand credit card, so purchases
earn best on a card with a strong online shopping multiplier or a dependable flat-rate
cashback rate.

stores.name: Benefit Cosmetics
stores.intro:
Benefit Cosmetics is a San Francisco founded beauty brand known for brow products,
mascara, and a playful, pink-branded retail presence, sold through its own boutiques,
website, and major beauty retailers like Sephora and Ulta. The brand has been owned by
LVMH since the early 1990s and remains one of the more recognizable names in prestige
drugstore-adjacent makeup. There's no Benefit Cosmetics co-brand credit card, so a card
that bonuses department store or general online shopping purchases is the better lever,
and buying through a department store account can sometimes stack with that retailer's own
loyalty program.

stores.name: Benjamin Moore
stores.intro:
Benjamin Moore is a premium U.S. paint manufacturer sold through more than 5,000
independent retailers including Aubuchon, Ace Hardware, and dedicated Benjamin Moore
shops. Because purchases happen at independent dealers, merchant category coding
varies, with some locations coding as home improvement and others as general
hardware retail.

When a dealer codes as home improvement, cards like the Wells Fargo Bilt and U.S.
Bank Cash+ can deliver elevated rewards on Aura, Regal Select, and Advance lines.
Ace Hardware locations selling Benjamin Moore can stack ACE Rewards points with
credit card cash back, while professional contractors with a Benjamin Moore PRO
account get tiered discounts that layer on top of any card rewards.

stores.name: Best Buy
stores.intro:
Best Buy is the largest U.S. specialty electronics retailer, with about 950 stores and a
strong bestbuy.com presence. In-store and online purchases generally code as electronics
or online shopping rather than as a department store, so the answer here usually comes
down to a flat online-retail card or a flat-rate card. The My Best Buy Visa offers
in-house financing and 5-6% back at Best Buy specifically, but the math only works for
shoppers spending several thousand dollars a year on electronics.

stores.name: Best Western
stores.intro:
Best Western Hotels & Resorts runs about 4,000 properties globally under a member-owned
cooperative structure that's unusual in the major-chain landscape. The portfolio spans
Best Western, Best Western Plus, and Best Western Premier on the core brand, with
Vīb, GLō, Aiden, Sadie, and Executive Residency rounding out boutique and extended-stay
segments, plus the SureStay budget brands. There's no Best Western co-brand credit
card in the U.S. market today, so the right play is a strong general hotel-category
card — Sapphire Reserve, Venture X, Amex Gold, or a flat-rate cashback card if you
prefer simplicity. Best Western Rewards points are earned only on direct bookings at
bestwestern.com or in-app; OTA stays earn nothing in the program.

stores.name: BevMo!
stores.intro: BevMo! is a West Coast beverage superstore selling wine, beer, and spirits in stores and for delivery. Spending here typically codes as a liquor or general retail merchant rather than as groceries, so supermarket multipliers will not trigger. Use a flat-rate card, or one with an online-shopping bonus for orders placed through its website.

stores.name: Big 5 Sporting Goods
stores.intro: Big 5 Sporting Goods runs neighborhood-format stores across the western US, stocking shoes, camping gear, and team-sport equipment that typically code as a department store. Grocery and dining multipliers will not apply to purchases here. Buying at big5sportinggoods.com generally codes as online shopping, so a flat-rate or online-shopping card tends to earn the most.

stores.name: Big Lots
stores.intro:
Big Lots is a closeout and discount retailer with roughly 1,300 stores across the U.S.,
selling furniture, seasonal goods, food and consumables, and an ever-rotating mix of
closeout merchandise. The chain went through bankruptcy and a restructuring under
Variety Wholesalers in 2025. Big Lots typically codes as a department store or
discount retailer for credit card networks. The Big Lots Credit Card (issued by
Comenity Capital Bank) offers store-only financing and points on Big Lots purchases,
though a general 2-5% department-store card from a major issuer is often more useful.

stores.name: Big O Tires
stores.intro: Big O Tires runs a nationwide network of franchised shops handling tires, alignments, and routine maintenance. Charges here usually code as online shopping or general auto retail rather than a travel or fuel category, and franchise coding can vary by location. Without a dedicated auto bonus on most cards, a strong flat-rate everyday card is typically the smart pick for tire and service tickets.

stores.name: Biggby Coffee
stores.intro: Biggby Coffee is a Michigan-founded franchise known for flavored lattes and an upbeat, locally owned drive-thru vibe across the Midwest and beyond. Purchases code as dining or restaurants on most cards, so reach for a card with a coffee or dining bonus. Reloading a Biggby gift card in advance may not always trigger the dining category, so paying directly is safest.

stores.name: Birkenstock
stores.intro: Birkenstock sells its cork-footbed sandals and clogs directly through birkenstock.com, and that online checkout typically codes as online shopping or general retail. The brand is centuries old and now publicly traded, with most US sales running through its own site and select retailers. Pair a card that rewards online purchases, since a sandals order rarely qualifies for grocery or dining bonuses.

stores.name: Bissell
stores.intro:
Bissell is a family-owned American floor-care brand making vacuums, carpet cleaners, and
cleaning solutions, sold direct at bissell.com and through mass and online retailers. There
is no Bissell co-brand credit card. For a direct purchase, a card with a strong online
shopping bonus or a flat-rate cashback card works best, while the same machine bought at a
big-box retailer can lean on that store's category rate.

stores.name: BJ's Restaurant
stores.intro: BJ's Restaurant & Brewhouse is a sit-down chain known for deep-dish pizza and its signature Pizookie dessert, and it is separate from the BJ's Wholesale warehouse club. Your check codes as dining, so the right tool is a card that rewards restaurant spending rather than a generic everyday card.

stores.name: BJ's Wholesale Club
stores.intro:
BJ's Wholesale Club is the East Coast-focused warehouse retailer with about 240 clubs
and a growing online catalog. Unlike Costco and Sam's Club, BJ's accepts manufacturer
coupons in addition to its own discounts, which can stack on top of card rewards. BJ's
accepts all four card networks, and BJ's gas pumps code as wholesale clubs — not as gas
— so a flat 5% gas card will not earn at the pump here.

stores.name: BLACK+DECKER
stores.intro:
BLACK+DECKER is a long-running power-tool and home-appliance brand owned by Stanley Black &
Decker, selling drills, yard tools, and small household appliances direct at
blackanddecker.com and through home-improvement and mass retailers. There is no BLACK+DECKER
co-brand credit card, so a direct purchase pairs best with a card that pays a bonus on home
improvement or online shopping, while the same tool bought at a hardware chain can earn
under that store's category rate.

stores.name: Blaze Pizza
stores.intro: Blaze Pizza popularized fast-fired, build-your-own personal pizzas baked in under a few minutes. As a fast-casual restaurant, your order codes as dining on nearly every card, so the better choice is one that rewards restaurant spending instead of a card that only pays the base rate.

stores.name: Blenders Eyewear
stores.intro:
Blenders Eyewear is a San Diego based sunglasses brand built around affordable,
polarized styles marketed heavily through social media and surf and skate culture. The
company sells almost exclusively direct to consumer through its own website, with
occasional pop-up and retail partnerships. There's no Blenders Eyewear co-brand credit
card, so a general online shopping rewards card or a flat-rate cashback card is the most
practical choice for a purchase here.

stores.name: Blick Art Materials
stores.intro: Blick Art Materials, also known as Dick Blick, is a leading US art-supply retailer serving artists and schools online and in stores. Online orders code as online shopping, while in-store buys usually ring up as general retail, with no art-supply bonus on mainstream cards. A flat-rate or online-shopping card is the practical pick.

stores.name: Bloomingdale's
stores.intro:
Bloomingdale's is a luxury-leaning department store with about 30 full-line locations
in the U.S., plus the higher-end Bloomingdale's by Bloomingdale's outlet stores. Most
shoppers are paying full price on apparel, beauty, and home goods, so the cashback rate
on your card matters more here than at a discount retailer.

Bloomingdale's accepts all four major networks (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) and
Apple Pay in stores. There is a co-branded Bloomingdale's American Express that earns
Loyallist points, but most general-purpose cards will pay you better in cashback unless
you spend several thousand dollars a year at Bloomingdale's specifically.

stores.name: Blue Apron
stores.intro:
Blue Apron pioneered the US meal-kit category in 2012 and remains a recognizable subscription
brand offering chef-designed recipes with pre-portioned ingredients shipped weekly, plus a
Heat & Eat line of prepared meals. The company was acquired by Wonder Group in 2023. Blue
Apron charges typically code as online retail or direct-marketing food, not in-store groceries
or restaurants, so dining and grocery category bonuses generally do not apply. The best
returns come from flat-rate cards (Citi Double Cash, Wells Fargo Active Cash, both at 2%).

stores.name: Blue Bottle Coffee
stores.intro: Blue Bottle Coffee is a specialty roaster, now Nestle-owned, with minimalist cafes concentrated in major US cities and a strong single-origin focus. In-cafe purchases code as dining or restaurants on most cards, so a dining bonus earns well. Bagged-bean and subscription orders from the website usually code as online shopping rather than dining.

stores.name: Blue Nile
stores.intro: Blue Nile pioneered buying engagement rings online, letting shoppers customize diamonds at bluenile.com, where checkout codes as online shopping or general retail. Ring purchases run large, so even a modest flat or online multiplier returns real value on a single order. Jewelry carries no dedicated bonus tier, making a high everyday-earning card the sensible choice here.

stores.name: Blueair
stores.intro:
Blueair is a Swedish air purifier brand known for HEPASilent filtration technology, selling
home and office air purifiers and replacement filters through its own site, online
marketplaces, and electronics and home goods retailers. Unilever acquired the company in
2021, folding it into its air and water treatment portfolio. There's no Blueair co-brand
credit card, so an electronics store bonus category card is the best fit for a purifier
purchase, with a general online shopping or flat-rate cashback card as the fallback.

stores.name: Bluemercury
stores.intro:
Bluemercury is a prestige beauty chain owned by Macy's Inc, with around 160 U.S.
boutiques plus bluemercury.com selling skincare, makeup, fragrance, and the in-house
M-61 and Lune+Aster brands. Direct purchases typically code as department store in
person and online shopping for web orders. Because Bluemercury is part of Macy's, the
free BlueRewards program is paired with Macy's Star Rewards and the Macy's
Citi-issued credit cards earn elevated points on Bluemercury spend.

stores.name: Bob's Discount Furniture
stores.intro:
Bob's Discount Furniture is a value-focused furniture chain with over 175 stores
across the Northeast, Mid-Atlantic, Midwest, and Southwest. The retailer is known
for its Goof Proof protection plan, free in-store Bob-O-Pedic samples, and pricing
that undercuts traditional furniture stores. Purchases code as general retail on
Visa, Mastercard, and Amex, not as home improvement.

Sectionals and bedroom sets at Bob's frequently run $1,500 to $3,000, which makes
the chain a strong venue for credit card signup bonus minimum spend. Flat-rate
earners like the Wells Fargo Active Cash and Citi Double Cash handle furniture spend
cleanly, while occasional Amex Offers on home retailers can stack additional
statement credits on top.

stores.name: Bobbi Brown
stores.intro:
Bobbi Brown Cosmetics is a prestige makeup and skincare brand owned by The Estee Lauder
Companies, sold direct at bobbibrowncosmetics.com and through Sephora, Ulta, and department
stores. There is no Bobbi Brown co-brand credit card, so a direct purchase codes as online
shopping, while the same items at a beauty retailer or department store can earn under that
store's category bonus.

stores.name: Bojangles
stores.intro:
Bojangles is a Southeast U.S. quick-service chain founded in Charlotte, North
Carolina in 1977, known for Cajun-seasoned fried chicken, made-from-scratch
biscuits, Bo-Berry Biscuits, and sweet tea. The chain operates over 800 restaurants
concentrated in the Carolinas, Tennessee, Virginia, and the broader Southeast.
Bojangles transactions code as restaurants on most credit card networks.

The Amex Gold pays 4x points on U.S. restaurants and is the top earner for daily
biscuit runs. The Chase Sapphire Preferred earns 3x on dining and pairs well with
travel redemptions. No-annual-fee dining cards including the Capital One SavorOne
at 3 percent and Wells Fargo Autograph at 3x are strong defaults. Bojangles also
runs the Bojangles Rewards program, layered on top of credit card cash back.

stores.name: Bombas
stores.intro:
Bombas is a direct-to-consumer sock and apparel brand built on a one-for-one donation
model, giving an item to homelessness relief organizations for every item purchased. The
company has expanded from its original performance socks into underwear, t-shirts, and
slippers, sold mainly through its own website with some retail partnerships. There's no
Bombas co-brand credit card, so a select clothing or general online shopping rewards card
is the most useful category for purchases here.

stores.name: Bonefish Grill
stores.intro:
Bonefish Grill is Bloomin' Brands' wood-grilled seafood concept (alongside Outback Steakhouse,
Carrabba's, and Fleming's) with about 175 US locations specializing in fresh fish flown in
several times a week, plus signature Bang Bang Shrimp and craft cocktails. Bonefish Grill
charges code as dining/restaurants on every card network, and Dine Rewards loyalty stacks
on top of credit card rewards. Cards with strong dining multipliers (Amex Gold 4x, Chase
Sapphire Preferred 3x, Capital One SavorOne 3% cash) earn at the full bonus on every visit.

stores.name: Bonobos
stores.intro:
Bonobos is a menswear brand best known for its fit-focused pants and its early Guideshop
model, where customers try on sizes in a showroom and have orders shipped rather than
carrying inventory on site. Originally a Walmart-owned brand, Bonobos was sold to Express
in 2023 and now sells through its own website and Express stores. There's no Bonobos
co-brand credit card, so a select clothing or general online shopping rewards card is the
better fit for purchases, though an Express store credit card, if applicable at checkout,
may offer its own separate rewards.

stores.name: Booking.com
stores.intro:
Booking.com is one of the largest global online travel agencies, owned by Booking
Holdings (which also owns Priceline, Kayak, and Agoda). Bookings code as travel
on every card we track. The honest catch: hotel chains typically do NOT credit
Booking.com stays toward elite-night or status counts, and many properties
exclude OTA stays from earning their loyalty points — so a Hilton/Marriott/Hyatt
loyalist almost always books direct. Booking's own Genius rewards tier and
occasional 10% off promos can still beat direct on price for one-off stays.

stores.name: Books-A-Million
stores.intro:
Books-A-Million, branded as BAM!, is the second-largest brick-and-mortar bookstore
chain in the United States with around 250 stores concentrated in the Southeast.
The retailer carries books, toys, gifts, magazines, and operates the Joe Muggs cafe
inside larger stores. BAM! purchases generally code as bookstore or general retail
rather than as a dedicated bonus category on most cards.

Cards with online shopping bonuses can earn better on booksamillion.com orders.
Bank of America Customized Cash and U.S. Bank Cash+ both offer selectable online
shopping categories worth 3 to 5 percent, and Chase Freedom Flex sometimes features
online shopping in its rotating quarters. The Millionaire's Club membership stacks
additional discounts with credit card cash back for frequent buyers.

stores.name: Bowflex
stores.intro:
Bowflex is a home fitness equipment brand known for its resistance-rod strength machines
and, more recently, connected bikes, treadmills, and adjustable dumbbells sold through its
own site and major retailers. The brand was acquired out of its former parent Nautilus by
Johnson Health Tech in 2022. There's no Bowflex co-brand credit card, so a general online
shopping rewards card or a flat-rate cashback card is the practical choice, and given the
price point of most equipment, a card with purchase protection or an extended warranty
benefit is worth checking too.

stores.name: BP
stores.intro:
BP and its Amoco-branded stations cover roughly 7,000 U.S. locations. Pumps code
cleanly as gas on every card we track. BP runs the BPme app and a separate BP
Visa with cash-back at BP/Amoco; in absolute cents most general-purpose 4-5% gas
cards beat it. The bigger swing for heavy BP buyers is the BPme Rewards loyalty
program, which stacks on top of any card's gas bonus.

stores.name: Brahmin
stores.intro:
Brahmin is an American leather goods brand known for its embossed handbags, wallets, and
accessories in signature reptile-textured patterns, made in a Massachusetts factory since
the 1980s. The line is sold direct online and through department stores and specialty
boutiques. There's no Brahmin co-brand credit card, so a department-store rewards card is
the better fit when buying through a wholesale partner, and a general online shopping or
flat-rate cashback card works best for direct orders.

stores.name: Braun
stores.intro:
Braun is a German consumer electronics brand best known for electric shavers, epilators,
and small kitchen appliances like hand blenders, sold through its own site, mass
retailers, and department stores. The company has been owned by Procter & Gamble since
2005, sitting alongside grooming brands like Gillette in P&G's portfolio. There's no Braun
co-brand credit card, so an electronics store bonus category card is the best fit for a
purchase, with a general online shopping or flat-rate cashback card as the fallback.

affiliate.offer: 15% off first purchase with code SMOOTH15

stores.name: Breeze Airways
stores.intro:
Breeze Airways is the low-cost startup launched by JetBlue founder David Neeleman in 2021,
flying a mix of Airbus A220-300s and Embraer E-Jets on underserved point-to-point routes
between small and mid-sized US cities. Breeze sells Nice, Nicer, and Nicest fare bundles
plus the premium Nicest cabin with extra-legroom seats. Breeze ticket purchases code as
airlines/airfare on every card. Cards with airfare bonuses (Chase Sapphire Reserve at 4x
on direct, Amex Gold at 3x on direct, Capital One Venture X at 2x base) all earn full rate.

stores.name: Brilliant Earth
stores.intro: Brilliant Earth focuses on ethically sourced and lab-grown diamonds, selling rings and fine jewelry at brilliantearth.com that code as online shopping or general retail. Because a ring is often a four or five figure purchase, the card you choose matters more than the category. Jewelry has no bonus multiplier, so a strong flat-rate or online-shopping card maximizes the reward.

stores.name: British Airways
stores.intro:
British Airways is the UK flag carrier and a oneworld founding member, hubbed at London
Heathrow with significant operations from London Gatwick and London City. The carrier's
Executive Club loyalty program (Avios) is a transfer partner of Amex Membership Rewards,
Chase Ultimate Rewards, Capital One miles, Bilt, and Citi ThankYou Points, which makes
Avios one of the most liquid airline currencies for transatlantic and short-haul oneworld
redemptions. British Airways tickets code as airlines/airfare, earning airfare bonuses fully.

stores.name: Brooks Brothers
stores.intro:
Brooks Brothers is the oldest apparel retailer in continuous operation in the U.S.,
founded in 1818 and known for tailored suits, dress shirts, and classic American
menswear, with a growing womenswear line. The brand went through Chapter 11 in 2020 and
is now owned and licensed by Authentic Brands Group, operating stores and an online shop
across the country. There's no Brooks Brothers co-brand credit card, so a select clothing
or general online shopping rewards card is the better fit for a purchase here.

stores.name: Buc-ee's
stores.intro:
Buc-ee's is a Texas-born travel center chain known for enormous footprints (often 50+
gas pumps and 50,000+ square feet of store), branded jerky and snacks, and a cult road
trip following. The chain has been expanding aggressively into the Southeast and
beyond. Fuel purchases at Buc-ee's pumps code as gas, while everything bought inside
(food, apparel, souvenirs) typically codes as convenience store or general
merchandise. Buc-ee's does not run its own credit card, so a strong gas-category card
is the cleanest way to earn rewards at the pump.

stores.name: Buckle
stores.intro:
Buckle is a denim-led specialty apparel chain with about 440 U.S. stores, heavily
weighted toward the Midwest and South and known for carrying premium jean brands. The
Buckle Black Card store credit card from Comenity offers rewards on Buckle purchases
and is paired with the free B-Rewards program. General-purpose cards usually code
Buckle as department store at the register and online shopping for buckle.com orders.

stores.name: Budget
stores.intro:
Budget is one of the largest car rental brands in the U.S., positioned as the value option
within Avis Budget Group alongside Avis and Payless, with airport and neighborhood
locations across the country and internationally. Its Fastbreak loyalty program lets
frequent renters skip the counter. There's no Budget co-brand credit card, so a card
that earns bonus rewards on car rentals or the broader travel category is the strongest
fit, and many travel cards also add supplemental rental car insurance as a card benefit
worth checking before declining the rental company's own coverage.

stores.name: Budget
stores.intro:
Budget is the value-priced sister brand to Avis under Avis Budget Group. Rentals
code as car rentals on every card we track. Pricing is generally a tier below Avis
for the same fleet, and the Avis Preferred program covers Budget rentals too.
General travel cards (Sapphire Preferred, Venture, Venture X) are the standard
pairing — most users book direct rather than via a card's travel portal because
Budget's coupon ecosystem (BCD codes, AAA, Costco) usually beats portal pricing.

stores.name: Budget Truck Rental
stores.intro:
Budget Truck Rental is the moving-truck division of Avis Budget Group, offering cargo
vans and box trucks for local and one-way moves through a network of dealer locations
across the U.S. It operates separately from the Budget car rental brand, though it shares
the same corporate parent and branding. There's no Budget Truck Rental co-brand credit
card, so a card that bonuses car rentals or the general travel category is the closest
fit, with a flat-rate cashback card as a reasonable fallback for a moving-truck charge.

stores.name: Buffalo Wild Wings
stores.intro:
Buffalo Wild Wings is a U.S. casual-dining chain with about 1,200 domestic
locations, focused on wings and sports bar atmosphere. Charges code as dining
on every card we track. Any dining-bonus card applies (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire
Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%). Blazin' Rewards in the app issues per-purchase
points redeemable for free items, stackable with card bonuses.

stores.name: Buffy
stores.intro:
Buffy is a direct-to-consumer bedding brand known for comforters made from recycled
plastic bottles and eucalyptus-fiber sheets, marketed around sustainability and sold
almost entirely through its own website. The company has expanded into pillows, throws,
and other bedroom textiles since launching in 2018. There's no Buffy co-brand credit
card, so a card that bonuses home goods purchases or a general online shopping rewards
card is the most useful category to lean on here.

stores.name: Build-A-Bear Workshop
stores.intro: Build-A-Bear Workshop lets customers assemble stuffed animals at mall workshops and online. Spending tends to code as a department store or general retail rather than as a toy-specific category, so grocery and dining bonuses will not apply. Use a flat-rate card or one that rewards online shopping for orders placed on its website.

stores.name: Bulova
stores.intro:
Bulova is an American watch brand founded in 1875 and now owned by Japan's Citizen Watch
Co., selling watches and clocks direct at bulova.com and through department stores and
authorized jewelers. There is no Bulova co-brand credit card, so a purchase codes as a
department store or online shopping charge. Because a watch is an occasional higher-dollar
buy, shoppers usually get the most from a card with a selectable department store category,
a strong online shopping multiplier, or a flat-rate cashback card for the one purchase.

stores.name: Bumble and Bumble
stores.intro:
Bumble and Bumble is a professional-grade hair care and styling brand that got its start
in a New York City salon in the 1970s, now owned by the Estee Lauder Companies and sold
through its own site, in salons, and at specialty beauty retailers like Sephora and Ulta.
There is no Bumble and Bumble co-brand credit card, so shoppers buying shampoo, styling
products, or tools online should lean on a card with a strong online shopping or general
beauty rewards category rather than looking for a store-specific option.

stores.name: Burger King
stores.intro:
Burger King is a U.S. fast-food chain with about 7,000 domestic locations, owned
by Restaurant Brands International. Charges code as dining on every card we track.
Standard dining-bonus cards (Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%) are
the obvious pairings. The Royal Perks loyalty program in the app issues "Crowns"
redeemable for free menu items, stackable with any card's bonus rate.

stores.name: Burlington
stores.intro:
Burlington is a U.S. off-price retail chain with about 1,000 stores selling
apparel, home, and baby goods at discount prices. Codes as department stores on
most cards. There's no co-branded Burlington credit card, so the strongest
pairings are department-store-bonus or flat-rate cashback cards. Burlington
doesn't run a high-volume loyalty program.

stores.name: Bushnell
stores.intro:
Bushnell is a long-running American optics brand known for binoculars, rifle scopes,
laser rangefinders, trail cameras, and golf rangefinders, popular with hunters, shooters,
and golfers alike. It carries no co-brand credit card of its own, so the better play for
a Bushnell purchase is a sporting goods category card or a general online shopping
rewards card, with a flat-rate cashback card as a reasonable fallback.

stores.name: buybuy BABY
