Orders typically code as online shopping or electronics retail, so a card with an online or general retail bonus tends to earn the most, and a shopping portal can stack additional value.

stores.name: LensCrafters
stores.intro: LensCrafters, part of EssilorLuxottica, runs eye exams and sells frames and lenses from major brands across hundreds of US locations. Buying online codes as online shopping and earns on a card with that bonus, but in-store purchases typically land at flat-rate retail. Vision insurance and FSA funds frequently apply, so compare those against the points you would earn.

stores.name: Les Schwab Tire Center
stores.intro: Les Schwab is a Pacific Northwest tire-center chain known for free flat repairs and rotations, with hundreds of company stores across the western US. Purchases tend to code as general retail or online shopping rather than fuel or travel, so dedicated bonus categories rarely apply. Since few cards reward tire service directly, your best flat-rate everyday card usually earns the most on a new set or an alignment.

stores.name: Levi's
stores.intro:
Levi's is the flagship denim brand of Levi Strauss & Co, sold through about 200 U.S.
company stores, levi.com, and a wide wholesale footprint at department stores and
specialty retailers. Direct purchases at Levi's stores or the website typically code
as department store or online shopping, while buying Levi's at Macy's or Kohl's codes
under that retailer. The free Levi's Red Tab membership stacks with credit card
rewards, and there is no Levi's branded credit card.

stores.name: Levoit
stores.intro:
Levoit is a home appliance brand under VeSync that specializes in air purifiers,
humidifiers, and small kitchen appliances aimed at the smart-home and wellness
market, sold mainly through its own site and online marketplaces. The brand has no
co-brand credit card. Purchases here fit best under a card that bonuses electronics
or general online shopping spend, or a flat-rate cashback card, since Levoit doesn't
fall into a home improvement or grocery category despite being a household product.

stores.name: Lidl
stores.intro:
Lidl is a German discount grocer that has expanded across the U.S. East Coast since
2017, with roughly 175 stores stretching from Georgia up to New York. The format is
small-footprint and private-label-heavy, comparable to Aldi. Lidl codes as a U.S.
supermarket for credit card networks, so grocery category bonuses (Amex Gold, Blue
Cash Preferred, Citi Custom Cash) earn their full multiplier. The chain accepts mobile
wallets and standard major networks.

stores.name: Life is Good
stores.intro:
Life is Good is a Boston-founded lifestyle apparel brand built around its optimistic
"Jake" stick-figure logo, selling t-shirts, hoodies, hats, and accessories through
its own stores, website, and wholesale partners, with a portion of profits directed
to its Life is Good Kids Foundation. The brand has no co-brand credit card. Shoppers
buying its casual apparel should default to a card that bonuses clothing or general
online shopping purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card if neither applies.

stores.name: Lindt
stores.intro:
Lindt is a Swiss chocolatier dating back to 1845, known worldwide for its Lindor
truffles and premium chocolate bars, sold through its own boutique shops, online
store, and a wide range of grocery and gift retailers. Lindt has no co-brand credit
card. Chocolate and gift purchases made directly through Lindt's website are best
earned with a general online shopping rewards card, or a flat-rate cashback card,
since confectionery purchases don't fall under a dedicated grocery or dining
category when bought online from the brand itself.

stores.name: Liquid I.V.
stores.intro:
Liquid I.V. is a hydration and wellness brand best known for its powdered
electrolyte drink mixes, sold in single-serve packets through its own site, major
retailers, and subscription orders. It has been owned by Unilever since 2020 and
carries no co-brand credit card. Purchases made directly through Liquid I.V.'s
website are best treated as general online shopping for rewards purposes, so a card
bonusing that category, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the practical option.

stores.name: Little Caesars
stores.intro:
Little Caesars is the third-largest pizza chain in the United States with more than 4,000
domestic locations, built around the Hot-N-Ready model that lets customers walk in and grab
a fresh pepperoni or cheese pizza without ordering ahead. The chain remains family-owned
by the Ilitch group. Little Caesars transactions, whether in-store, drive-thru Pizza Portal
pickup, or app delivery, all code as dining/restaurants. Top dining cards earn full bonus
here: Amex Gold at 4x, Chase Sapphire Preferred at 3x, Capital One SavorOne at 3% cash.

stores.name: Little Sleepies
stores.intro:
Little Sleepies is a direct-to-consumer kids and baby apparel brand built around
bamboo viscose pajamas, rompers, and loungewear sold in matching family sets and
frequent limited-edition prints. The company sells almost exclusively through its
own website rather than department stores, which makes it a straightforward
online-shopping purchase. There is no Little Sleepies co-brand credit card, so the
best approach is a card that pays an elevated rate on online or general retail
purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card if simplicity matters more than optimizing
by category.

stores.name: Little Tikes
stores.intro:
Little Tikes is a classic American toy brand owned by MGA Entertainment, making playhouses,
slides, ride-ons, and outdoor play sets, sold direct at littletikes.com and through toy and
mass retailers. There is no Little Tikes co-brand credit card, so a direct purchase earns
best on a card with a strong online shopping multiplier or a flat-rate cashback rate.

stores.name: Living Spaces
stores.intro:
Living Spaces is a West Coast furniture chain with around 40 large-format showrooms
across California, Texas, Arizona, Nevada, and a growing East Coast footprint. The
retailer is known for in-stock sofas with same-week delivery and a wide rug
assortment. Furniture purchases code as general retail, not as a home improvement
bonus category on most rewards cards.

Because Living Spaces orders frequently run into four figures, sofas and bedroom
sets are excellent for clearing signup bonus minimum spend. Cards with strong base
earn rates, including the Citi Double Cash and Capital One Venture X, are the
practical default. Living Spaces also offers in-house financing through Synchrony for
promotional zero-interest terms.

stores.name: LL Flooring
stores.intro:
LL Flooring, formerly known as Lumber Liquidators, is a flooring specialty retailer
with over 400 U.S. stores selling hardwood, bamboo, vinyl plank, laminate, and tile
flooring along with installation supplies. The chain typically codes as home
improvement on most credit card networks, which makes it eligible for cards that
carry a home improvement bonus category.

Cards like the U.S. Bank Cash+ and Bank of America Customized Cash can route LL
Flooring purchases into a selectable bonus, while the Chase Freedom Flex sometimes
features home improvement in its 5 percent rotating quarters. Whole-house flooring
projects are large enough to clear most credit card signup bonus minimums in a
single transaction.

stores.name: Lodge Cast Iron
stores.intro:
Lodge Cast Iron is a family-owned American cookware maker known for its pre-seasoned
skillets and Dutch ovens, sold direct at lodgecastiron.com and through home and mass
retailers. There is no Lodge co-brand credit card, so a direct purchase earns best on a card
with an online shopping bonus or a flat-rate cashback rate, while the same pan bought at a
home-goods store can lean on that retailer's category.

stores.name: Loews Hotels
stores.intro: Loews Hotels is a US-based upscale chain with properties in major cities and resort destinations, including hotels tied to Universal Orlando. Stays code as a hotel or travel purchase, the territory of cards carrying a hotel or general travel bonus. Booking direct often preserves loyalty benefits, while a travel portal can sometimes earn more points but may forfeit elite perks.

stores.name: LOFT
stores.intro: LOFT, a sister brand to Ann Taylor, sells more casual and relaxed women's clothing. Online checkout codes as online shopping or retail, so a shopping portal or rotating bonus may apply, while in-store purchases generally earn the base rate since clothing rarely qualifies for a category bonus.

stores.name: Logitech
stores.intro:
Logitech is a Swiss-American maker of computer peripherals and accessories, from mice and
keyboards to webcams, gaming gear under Logitech G, and streaming equipment, sold direct at
logitech.com and through electronics and online retailers. There is no Logitech co-brand
credit card, so purchases earn best on a card with a strong online shopping multiplier, or
at a retailer like Best Buy whose electronics category bonus can apply.

stores.name: Long John Silver's
stores.intro: Long John Silver's is the dominant US fast-food seafood chain, built around battered fish, hushpuppies, and a nautical theme. Orders code as dining or restaurants on most cards, so reach for a dining multiplier. Some locations are co-branded with A and W or other chains, but the charge still posts under the restaurant code.

stores.name: LongHorn Steakhouse
stores.intro:
LongHorn Steakhouse is Darden Restaurants' Western-themed steak concept with about 580 US
locations, sitting just below the chain's Capital Grille tier and offering fire-grilled steaks
like the Outlaw Ribeye and Flo's Filet plus chicken, salmon, and ribs. LongHorn participates
in the cross-Darden eGift card program with Olive Garden and others. LongHorn transactions
code as dining/restaurants. Cards with strong dining multipliers (Amex Gold 4x, Chase
Sapphire Preferred 3x, Capital One SavorOne 3% cash) earn full rate on every steak dinner.

stores.name: LOOKFANTASTIC
stores.intro:
LOOKFANTASTIC is a UK-founded online beauty retailer selling skincare, makeup,
haircare, and fragrance from a large mix of prestige and niche brands, with a U.S.
storefront and its well-known monthly subscription beauty box. It operates under THG
plc and has no co-brand credit card. Since it functions as a pure online beauty
marketplace rather than a physical retail chain, a card that bonuses general online
shopping purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the best way to earn rewards on
orders here.

stores.name: Love's Travel Stops
stores.intro:
Love's Travel Stops runs more than 650 locations across 42 states, serving both
passenger drivers and commercial truckers with fuel, food, showers, and tire service.
Standard fuel pumps code as gas, so any gas category bonus card will earn its full
multiplier at the pump. Inside-store food and merchandise can code as convenience
store or restaurant depending on the format. The MyLove's Rewards program offers
per-gallon discounts and points redeemable on food and showers, which stacks
cleanly with a separate gas rewards card.

stores.name: Lowe's
stores.intro:
Lowe's is the second-largest U.S. home-improvement retailer at about 1,750 stores, with
a long-standing rivalry with Home Depot for both consumer and pro spend. Pricing is
similar between the two, so the right card here is whichever home-improvement-bonus
general-purpose card you carry, plus the Lowe's Advantage credit line if you're
financing a large project. Lowe's codes as home improvement in-store and on lowes.com.

stores.name: Lucky Brand
stores.intro:
Lucky Brand is a California-born denim and casual apparel label known for its
vintage-inspired jeans, along with tees, jackets, and accessories. The brand sells
through its own stores and site as well as inside major department stores like
Macy's and Dillard's, so purchases can land in either the online-shopping or
department-store bucket depending on where you check out. There is no Lucky
Brand co-brand credit card, so shoppers are better served leaning on a card that
rewards online retail or department-store spending, or a general flat-rate
cashback card for everyday simplicity.

stores.name: Lucky Strike
stores.intro:
Lucky Strike Entertainment operates a national chain of upscale bowling, arcade,
and dining venues under the Lucky Strike name, part of the same company behind
Bowlero-branded centers. Locations combine bowling lanes with bars, lounges, and
event space aimed at both casual outings and private parties. There is no Lucky
Strike co-brand credit card, so the most useful play is a card that earns a
bonus rate on entertainment purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card for
straightforward everyday spending on an outing there.

stores.name: Lufthansa
stores.intro: Lufthansa is Germany's flagship carrier and a founding Star Alliance member, flying extensive routes between the US and Europe. Tickets and onboard purchases code as an airline or travel charge, rewarded by cards with an airline or broad travel bonus. Buying through a card issuer's travel portal can earn extra points, while booking direct better protects Miles and More credit and elite status.

stores.name: Lululemon
stores.intro:
Lululemon is the dominant U.S. athletic-apparel brand with about 700 stores and a heavy
lululemon.com presence. Like other apparel retailers, Lululemon doesn't fall into a card
bonus category in-store — the right answer there is a strong flat-rate cashback card.
Online purchases at lululemon.com code as online shopping, which opens up a different
set of category bonuses.

stores.name: Lumens Light + Living
stores.intro:
Lumens Light + Living is an online retailer specializing in designer lighting
fixtures, furniture, and home decor, carrying hundreds of brands ranging from
boutique studios to well-known modern design houses. It functions as a curated
marketplace rather than a manufacturer, shipping direct to consumers nationwide.
There is no Lumens co-brand credit card, so a home-improvement category card or
a card with strong online-shopping rewards is the better fit for larger lighting
or furniture purchases, with a flat-rate cashback card as a simple fallback.

stores.name: Lush
stores.intro:
Lush Fresh Handmade Cosmetics is a British beauty brand best known for bath bombs,
shampoo bars, and packaging-light skincare. The U.S. footprint covers about 200 mall
and street-front stores plus lushusa.com. Direct purchases typically code as
department store in person and online shopping for web orders, while the free Lush
Rewards program offers points and member perks that stack with credit card earnings.
Lush does not run a U.S. branded credit card.

stores.name: Lyft
stores.intro:
Lyft is the second-largest U.S. rideshare service after Uber. Rides code under
the transit category on most cards. The Chase Sapphire Reserve historically pays
10x on Lyft rides through 2027, and Lyft Pink (Lyft's premium membership) is
comped with several Chase cards. Sapphire Preferred, Venture X, and SavorOne all
earn elevated transit/rideshare rates without the Lyft-specific multipliers.

stores.name: MAC Cosmetics
stores.intro:
MAC Cosmetics is a prestige color cosmetics brand owned by The Estée Lauder Companies,
sold through about 100 U.S. freestanding stores, maccosmetics.com, and a wide
department store and Ulta wholesale footprint. Direct purchases at MAC stores or the
website typically code as department store or online shopping, while MAC bought at
Macy's or Ulta codes under that retailer. The free MAC Lover loyalty program offers
tiered points and stacks with credit card multipliers.

stores.name: Macy's
stores.intro:
Macy's is the largest U.S. mid-tier department store, with about 500 full-line stores
plus Backstage outlets and a heavy promotional calendar. Most shoppers buy at heavy
discount, so the cashback rate stacks on already-reduced prices. Macy's codes as a
department store at the register and as online shopping on macys.com, which means cards
bonusing on either category can earn here. The Macy's American Express has its own
Loyallist points layer for shoppers doing several thousand a year at Macy's
specifically.

stores.name: Madewell
stores.intro:
Madewell is the denim-led casualwear brand spun out of J.Crew, with about 140 U.S.
stores and a strong DTC site at madewell.com. The Madewell Insider loyalty program is
free and offers a denim recycling perk for old jeans. General-purpose credit cards
typically code Madewell as department store in person and online shopping for web
checkouts, and the brand does not currently have its own branded credit card.

stores.name: Madison Reed
stores.intro:
Madison Reed makes ammonia-free, salon-quality hair color kits sold direct to
consumers online and through its own network of hair color bar salons in select
cities. The company built its business around at-home color subscriptions as an
alternative to traditional salon visits. There is no Madison Reed co-brand
credit card, so shoppers should rely on a card that rewards online retail
purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card for a simpler approach to everyday
beauty spending.

stores.name: Maggiano's Little Italy
stores.intro:
Maggiano's Little Italy, owned by Brinker International (Chili's parent), runs about 50
upscale-casual Italian restaurants known for family-style portions, the Chocolate Zuccotto
Cake, and the Classic Pastas program where guests take home a second pasta with each entree.
Maggiano's transactions code as dining/restaurants on every credit card. With higher check
averages than typical casual dining, top dining cards meaningfully boost return: Amex Gold
at 4x, Chase Sapphire Reserve at 4x, Chase Sapphire Preferred at 3x, Capital One Savor at 3%.

stores.name: Manscaped
stores.intro:
Manscaped is a men's grooming brand best known for its below-the-waist trimmers
and body grooming tools, alongside skincare and hygiene products it built into a
broader personal-care lineup. The company sells primarily through its own site
and app, with a subscription refill option for blades and grooming supplies.
There is no Manscaped co-brand credit card, so the sensible approach is a card
that earns extra on online shopping, or a flat-rate cashback card for
straightforward everyday purchases.

stores.name: Marathon
stores.intro:
Marathon-branded stations cover about 7,000 U.S. locations primarily across the
Midwest and Southeast. Pumps code cleanly as gas on every card we track. The
MakeItCount Rewards program offers cents-per-gallon savings that stack with
whatever card you swipe; absolute cashback usually wins with a 4-5% general gas
card rather than a Marathon-only co-brand.

stores.name: Marc Jacobs
stores.intro:
Marc Jacobs is an American fashion house known for ready-to-wear apparel,
handbags, and fragrances, with the Tote Bag and Daisy perfume line among its
most recognizable products. The brand, owned by LVMH parent company overseen
by designer Marc Jacobs himself, sells through its own boutiques and site as
well as through department stores like Nordstrom, Macy's, and Bloomingdale's.
There is no Marc Jacobs co-brand credit card, so a card that rewards online
shopping or department-store purchases is the better fit, with a flat-rate
cashback card as a simple alternative.

stores.name: Marco's Pizza
stores.intro:
Marco's Pizza, founded in Ohio in 1978, runs about 1,200 US locations with a delivery and
carryout model emphasizing fresh dough made in-store, three-cheese blends, and a private
pizza sauce recipe. The chain has grown rapidly across the South and Midwest. Marco's
transactions code as dining/restaurants whether you order at the counter, online, or through
the Marco's Rewards app. Dining-bonus cards earn their full rate here: Amex Gold pays 4x,
Chase Sapphire Preferred pays 3x, and the Capital One SavorOne pays 3% unlimited cash back.

stores.name: Mariano's
stores.intro: Mariano's is Kroger's Chicago-market grocer, famous for its in-store oyster bars, gelato counters, and wine selections. Standard shopping codes as groceries on most cards, making a supermarket bonus the best fit here. Prepared-food and cafe items bought at the grocery checkout generally still ride the same grocery code rather than dining.

stores.name: Mario Badescu
stores.intro:
Mario Badescu is a New York skincare brand built around its longtime Manhattan
salon, known for affordable, dermatologist-informed products like its Facial
Spray and Drying Lotion that developed a cult following well beyond the original
storefront. The brand sells direct online and through beauty retailers such as
Ulta. There is no Mario Badescu co-brand credit card, so a card that earns a
bonus on online shopping is the better lever for these purchases, or a
flat-rate cashback card for everyday simplicity.

stores.name: Marks and Spencer
stores.intro:
Marks and Spencer is a long-running British retailer selling clothing, home
goods, and food, with a U.S.-facing online storefront that ships internationally
for shoppers outside the UK. The company is one of Britain's best-known
department-store chains, though it operates no physical U.S. locations. There
is no Marks and Spencer co-brand credit card in the U.S. market, so a card that
rewards department-store or general online shopping is the more useful choice,
with a flat-rate cashback card as a simple fallback.

stores.name: Marriott
stores.intro:
Marriott Bonvoy is the world's largest hotel loyalty program, covering more than 30
brands from budget Fairfield Inn through luxury Ritz-Carlton, St. Regis, and W Hotels.
Direct stays at marriott.com or any Bonvoy property earn the most points and unlock
elite-night credits, but the math gets nuanced: co-branded Marriott cards layer extra
Bonvoy points on top of base earnings, while general hotel-category cards (Sapphire
Reserve, Venture X) often yield higher cashback-equivalent value if you don't care
about Bonvoy status. Stays booked through OTAs like Expedia or Hotels.com forfeit
points and elite credit, so the in-program booking is almost always the right call.

stores.name: Marshalls
stores.intro:
Marshalls is an off-price retail chain owned by TJX Companies, with about 1,200
U.S. stores selling apparel, home, and accessories at discount prices. Codes as
department stores on most cards. The TJX Rewards Mastercard offers 5% back at
Marshalls, TJ Maxx, HomeGoods, Sierra, and Homesense — typically the best rate
for heavy TJX shoppers. Otherwise pair with a department-store-bonus card or a
flat-rate cashback card.

stores.name: Master Lock
stores.intro:
Master Lock is a longtime American manufacturer of padlocks, combination locks,
and security hardware, a category it has dominated since the 1920s and that now
extends to bike locks, cable locks, and lock boxes sold through hardware stores
and its own site. There is no Master Lock co-brand credit card, so purchases
are best routed through a home-improvement category card, or a flat-rate
cashback card if you would rather not track category bonuses for a lower-spend
item like this.

stores.name: Mattress Firm
stores.intro:
Mattress Firm is the largest U.S. mattress specialty chain with more than 2,300
stores, carrying Sealy, Serta, Tempur-Pedic, Purple, and its private-label brands.
Mattress purchases code as general retail on most credit card networks, which means
no automatic 5 percent home improvement bonus and no grocery category overlap.

King-size mattress sets routinely cross $2,000, making Mattress Firm useful for
clearing signup bonus minimums on cards like the Amex Gold or Chase Sapphire
Preferred. The retailer also offers Synchrony-issued financing with promotional
zero-interest windows. Paying with a 2 percent flat-rate card such as the Citi
Double Cash and avoiding interest charges typically produces a better net outcome.

stores.name: Maui Jim
stores.intro:
Maui Jim is a Hawaii-founded sunglasses brand built around its proprietary
polarized lens technology, marketed as reducing glare more effectively than
standard polarized options. The company sells premium sunglasses direct online
and through optical retailers and specialty shops nationwide. There is no Maui
Jim co-brand credit card, so a card that rewards online shopping is the more
useful pick for a direct purchase, or a flat-rate cashback card for a simpler
approach.

stores.name: Maurices
stores.intro: Maurices serves small-town and mid-size markets with affordable women's fashion across many US shopping centers. Its online store codes as online shopping or retail, where a rotating or portal bonus can help, while store purchases tend to fall to flat-rate since apparel is rarely a bonus category.

stores.name: Maverik
stores.intro:
Maverik, branded "Adventure's First Stop," is a Mountain West convenience-store and
fuel chain with around 400 locations spread across Utah, Idaho, Wyoming, Nevada,
Colorado, Arizona, and surrounding states. Fuel pumps code as gas, so any gas category
rewards card will earn its full bonus. The Adventure Club loyalty program (and the
related Adventure Club Visa, issued via First Bankcard) adds per-gallon discounts and
elevated earn on Maverik in-store purchases including the chain's BonFire food
program.

stores.name: Mavis Discount Tire
stores.intro: Mavis Discount Tire has grown into one of the largest independent tire chains on the East Coast, offering tires plus brakes, alignments, and oil changes. Purchases usually code as general retail or online shopping rather than a gas or travel category, so bonus categories rarely fire. A reliable flat-rate everyday card is generally the best earner on tires and service work here.

stores.name: Max
stores.intro:
Max is Warner Bros. Discovery's streaming service, the rebranded successor to
HBO Max. Monthly subscriptions code as streaming on every card we track.
Streaming-bonus cards (Blue Cash Preferred 6%, U.S. Bank Cash+ 5% on selected
categories) are the strongest pairings. Max also bundles with Hulu/Disney+ in
some seasonal Disney Bundle promotions.

stores.name: Maytag
stores.intro:
Maytag is a longstanding American major appliance brand covering washers,
dryers, dishwashers, and kitchen appliances, positioned around dependability
since its "lonely repairman" advertising era. The brand has been owned by
Whirlpool since 2006 and sells through its own site as well as home-improvement
and appliance retailers. There is no Maytag co-brand credit card, so a
home-improvement category card is the natural fit for an appliance purchase,
or a flat-rate cashback card for shoppers who prefer not to track categories.

stores.name: McDonald's
stores.intro:
McDonald's is the largest U.S. quick-service restaurant chain at about 13,500 locations.
All purchases — in-store, drive-thru, and via the McDonald's app — code as
dining/restaurants on every card we track, so any 3-5% dining-category card earns here.
The McDonald's app's MyMcDonald's Rewards program stacks on top of card cashback.

stores.name: Meijer
stores.intro:
Meijer is a Midwest superstore chain with about 240 locations across Michigan,
Ohio, Indiana, Illinois, Kentucky, and Wisconsin — selling groceries, household,
and general merchandise under one roof. Most cards code Meijer as groceries (it's
one of the few "supercenter" chains that does), so a 6% Blue Cash Preferred or
3% SavorOne earns the bonus on Meijer's full basket including non-grocery items.
mPerks runs separate digital coupons that stack on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Melissa & Doug
stores.intro: Melissa & Doug is known for wooden toys, puzzles, and craft kits sold online and through retail partners. Direct orders on its site generally code as online shopping, and there is no dedicated toy bonus category on most cards. A flat-rate card or one with an online-shopping multiplier is the practical choice for gifts and restocks.

stores.name: Menards
stores.intro:
Menards is a Midwest-focused home-improvement chain with about 350 stores, well-known
for its 11% mail-in rebate promotions that effectively act as in-house cashback on top
of card rewards. The math here is interesting: an 11% Menards rebate stacked with a 2%
flat-rate cashback card often beats most home-improvement category cards on net. Menards
codes as home improvement at the register and on menards.com.

stores.name: Mercari
stores.intro:
Mercari is a Japan-founded peer-to-peer marketplace where individual users list and
ship secondhand and new items across electronics, apparel, collectibles, and home
goods. The U.S. arm operates similarly to eBay but with a simpler flat-fee structure
and shipping label integration. Mercari purchases code as online shopping for credit
card networks, so an online-shopping category bonus card will earn its multiplier on
buyer-side transactions. Mercari does not issue a co-brand card.

stores.name: MeUndies
stores.intro:
MeUndies is a direct-to-consumer underwear and loungewear brand built around a
monthly subscription model, known for colorful prints, modal fabric, and
limited-edition designer collaborations. The company sells almost entirely
through its own website and app rather than physical retail. There is no
MeUndies co-brand credit card, so a card that earns a bonus on online shopping
is the better fit, or a flat-rate cashback card for a simpler approach to
subscription-style purchases.

stores.name: MGM Resorts
stores.intro: MGM Resorts operates major casino hotels on the Las Vegas Strip and beyond, including Bellagio and MGM Grand. Lodging charges usually code as a hotel or travel purchase, though casino, gaming, and some resort transactions can code differently and may not earn travel bonuses. Use a hotel or travel card for the room, and watch for cash-advance coding on any gaming-related charges.

stores.name: Michael Kors
stores.intro:
Michael Kors is the flagship accessible luxury brand under Capri Holdings, which also
owns Versace and Jimmy Choo. The U.S. footprint includes hundreds of Lifestyle and
outlet stores plus michaelkors.com selling handbags, watches, footwear, and ready to
wear. Direct purchases typically code as department store in person and online
shopping for web orders, while MK bought at department stores codes under the host
retailer. The free KORSVIP loyalty program stacks with credit card earnings.

stores.name: Michaels
stores.intro:
Michaels is the largest U.S. arts-and-crafts retailer with about 1,300 stores,
selling beads, yarn, frames, and seasonal decor. In-store charges code as
department stores or specialty retail; michaels.com codes as online shopping.
The Michaels Rewards program is free and issues per-spend points redeemable
for in-store credit. Pair with a flat-rate cashback or department-store-bonus
card; there's no widely-recommended Michaels-specific credit card.

stores.name: Micro Center
stores.intro:
Micro Center is a specialty computer and electronics retailer with around 25 large
U.S. stores known for in-stock PC components, aggressive CPU and motherboard combo
pricing, and a deep selection of GPUs, 3D printers, and DIY parts. Most credit cards
code Micro Center as electronics or general retail rather than as a dedicated
category, so flat-rate cards often do the heavy lifting.

Online orders through microcenter.com qualify for online shopping bonuses on the
Bank of America Customized Cash, Chase Freedom Flex when online shopping is the
rotating quarter, and U.S. Bank Cash+. Custom PC builds can run thousands of
dollars, making Micro Center a useful target for clearing signup bonus minimums.

stores.name: Microsoft Store
stores.intro:
The Microsoft Store is Microsoft's online retail destination for Surface laptops,
Xbox consoles and games, Windows software, Microsoft 365 subscriptions, and Office
products. Following the closure of all physical Microsoft Stores in 2020, all U.S.
consumer shopping happens through microsoft.com/store. Purchases code as online
shopping or software on most credit card networks.

Cards with online shopping bonuses can deliver strong returns on Surface and Xbox
purchases. The Bank of America Customized Cash, U.S. Bank Cash+, and Chase Freedom
Flex rotating quarters can all push these orders to 5 percent back. Microsoft 365
and Xbox Game Pass recurring subscriptions may code as software or streaming
services, which can trigger streaming category bonuses on cards like the Amex Blue
Cash Preferred.

stores.name: Midas
stores.intro:
Midas is a global auto service franchise with around 1,200 U.S. locations
specializing in exhaust, brakes, tires, suspension, and routine maintenance. Most
Midas franchisees code transactions as auto services on Visa, Mastercard, and Amex
networks, which typically falls outside dedicated rewards categories on consumer
cards.

Big-ticket brake jobs and exhaust work often run $500 to $1,500, which is enough
to make MID category selection worthwhile on cards like Citi Custom Cash when auto
services is the top monthly category. U.S. Bank Cash+ also offers wider auto
categories. For everyday simplicity, the Wells Fargo Active Cash and Citi Double
Cash pay a clean 2 percent on every Midas invoice.

stores.name: Milani Cosmetics
stores.intro:
Milani Cosmetics is a Los Angeles-based makeup brand built around affordable,
drugstore-priced products, with its Baked Blush and Conceal + Perfect line among
its best-known items. The brand sells direct online along with wide distribution
through mass retailers like Target, Walmart, and Ulta. There is no Milani
co-brand credit card, so a card that rewards online shopping is the better lever
for a direct purchase, or a flat-rate cashback card if you would rather keep
things simple.

stores.name: Milk Bar
stores.intro:
Milk Bar is the dessert brand founded by pastry chef Christina Tosi, known for
Crack Pie, Compost Cookies, and layered "truffle" cakes sold through a chain of
cafe locations and nationwide shipping of frozen treats. It grew out of the
Momofuku restaurant group before becoming an independent company. There is no
Milk Bar co-brand credit card, so a dining-category card fits an in-store
purchase while a card with online-shopping rewards suits a shipped order, or a
flat-rate cashback card for a simpler option.

stores.name: Milk Makeup
stores.intro:
Milk Makeup is a cosmetics brand known for multi-use, skin-forward products
like its cream sticks and Hydro Grip primer, marketed toward a younger,
low-maintenance beauty audience. The brand originated inside the Milk Studios
photography and events company before spinning out and is now owned by e.l.f.
Beauty. There is no Milk Makeup co-brand credit card, so a card that rewards
online shopping is the better fit for a direct purchase, or a flat-rate
cashback card for everyday simplicity.

stores.name: Mint Mobile
stores.intro:
Mint Mobile is a prepaid wireless carrier that runs on T-Mobile's network,
known for selling multi-month plans upfront at a discounted effective monthly
rate. Actor Ryan Reynolds was a co-owner before T-Mobile acquired the company
outright. There is no Mint Mobile co-brand credit card, so the best angle is a
card that earns an elevated rate on phone or cell-phone-provider purchases, or
a flat-rate cashback card since Mint's own pricing already keeps the bill low.

stores.name: Minted
stores.intro:
Minted is an online marketplace that sells stationery, art prints, wedding
invitations, and home decor designed by a community of independent artists and
designers, with pieces selected through crowdsourced design competitions. The
company operates purely as an e-commerce platform with no physical retail
presence. There is no Minted co-brand credit card, so a card that rewards
online shopping purchases is the better fit, or a flat-rate cashback card for
a simpler approach.

stores.name: Misfits Market
stores.intro:
Misfits Market is an online grocery delivery service that started by selling
"ugly" or surplus produce at a discount to reduce food waste, and has since
expanded into a broader grocery box covering pantry staples, meat, and dairy
shipped direct to subscribers' homes. There is no Misfits Market co-brand
credit card, so a groceries-category card is the natural fit for these
purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card if you would rather not track
categories.

stores.name: Mizuno
stores.intro:
Mizuno is a Japanese sporting goods company with a long history in running
shoes, baseball and softball gear, golf equipment, and volleyball apparel,
particularly well known among competitive runners and ballplayers for its
performance footwear. The brand sells direct online and through specialty
sporting goods retailers. There is no Mizuno co-brand credit card, so a
sporting-goods category card is the better fit, or a flat-rate cashback card
for a simpler approach to gear purchases.

stores.name: MOD Pizza
stores.intro: MOD Pizza runs a similar build-your-own, fast-fired model with one flat price regardless of toppings. Orders code as dining or restaurants on most cards, so pulling out a card with a restaurant bonus earns more on each personal pizza than a plain everyday card would.

stores.name: Moe's Southwest Grill
stores.intro:
Moe's Southwest Grill, owned by Focus Brands, runs about 600 fast-casual locations centered
on burritos, bowls, quesadillas, and the chain's signature free chips and salsa with every
entree. Every Moe's transaction, whether in-store, drive-thru, or through the Moe's Rewards
app, codes as a dining/restaurant charge. Pair a top dining card here for the best return:
Amex Gold pays 4x Membership Rewards, Chase Sapphire Preferred pays 3x Ultimate Rewards,
and Capital One SavorOne pays 3% cash back on every Welcome to Moe's order.

stores.name: Moen
stores.intro:
Moen is a major American manufacturer of kitchen and bathroom faucets, sinks,
and showering fixtures, widely sold through home-improvement retailers like
Home Depot and Lowe's as well as its own site. The brand is owned by Fortune
Brands Innovations and is a common fixture choice in both new construction and
renovation projects. There is no Moen co-brand credit card, so a
home-improvement category card is the natural fit for a fixture purchase, or a
flat-rate cashback card for a simpler approach.

stores.name: Molton Brown
stores.intro:
Molton Brown is a British luxury bath, body, and fragrance brand owned by Kao Corporation,
sold direct at moltonbrown.com and through prestige department stores. There is no Molton
Brown co-brand credit card, so a purchase codes as online shopping, and a card with a strong
online shopping bonus or a flat-rate cashback rate earns the most.

stores.name: Moroccanoil
stores.intro:
Moroccanoil is a hair care brand built around its original argan oil treatment,
which grew into a full line of shampoos, conditioners, and styling products
used widely in professional salons as well as sold direct to consumers. The
brand is distributed through salons, beauty retailers, and its own website.
There is no Moroccanoil co-brand credit card, so a card that rewards online
shopping is the better fit for a direct purchase, or a flat-rate cashback card
for a simpler approach to beauty spending.

stores.name: Motel 6
stores.intro:
Motel 6 operates roughly 1,400 budget motels across the US and Canada, distinguishable by
the We'll leave the light on for you tagline and consistently low nightly rates that have
anchored the chain at the bottom of the lodging price spectrum since 1962. Motel 6 was
acquired by Oyo's parent in 2025. Sister brand Studio 6 offers extended-stay options. Motel 6
charges code as lodging/hotels on every card. Hotel-bonus cards earn full rate: Chase
Sapphire Reserve at 4x direct, Capital One Venture X at 2x base, Amex Platinum at 5x prepaid.

stores.name: Movado
stores.intro:
Movado is a Swiss watchmaker best known for the Museum Dial design, a minimalist face
featuring a single dot at 12 o'clock that has been part of MoMA's permanent design
collection. The Movado Group also owns or licenses other watch brands, including Concord,
Ebel, and various fashion-brand licensed lines, and its watches are sold both direct at
movado.com and through department stores and jewelry retailers. There is no Movado
co-brand credit card, so shoppers buying a Movado watch are best served by a card that
earns strong rewards on department-store or general online-shopping purchases, or a
flat-rate cashback card for a big-ticket item like a watch.

stores.name: Munchkin
stores.intro:
Munchkin is a baby and toddler products company known for items like the Miracle 360
sippy cup, bath toys, feeding gear, and diaper-disposal systems, sold at munchkin.com
as well as through major retailers like Target and Walmart. It's a private, independent
brand with no credit card of its own. Since Munchkin purchases are usually made online
or as part of a broader baby-registry shop, a card that rewards online shopping or
offers flat-rate cashback is the simplest way to earn on these purchases, rather than
looking for a category-specific multiplier that doesn't really exist for baby gear.

stores.name: Murphy USA
stores.intro:
Murphy USA runs about 1,700 low-price gas and convenience stations across the U.S.,
many of them sited in Walmart parking lots under the Murphy USA or Murphy Express
banners. The chain competes primarily on price per gallon. Fuel codes as gas at the
pump, so any gas category bonus card will earn its full multiplier. The Murphy Drive
Rewards loyalty app and Murphy USA Visa add per-gallon discounts that can stack with a
major-issuer gas card if used carefully.

stores.name: Naked Wines
stores.intro:
Naked Wines is a direct-to-consumer wine retailer built around funding independent
winemakers, where members pay a monthly deposit toward future bottle purchases at
member-only pricing. It ships wine straight to customers' doors in states where that's
legal, positioning itself against traditional wine shops and big-box wine sections.
There's no Naked Wines credit card, and grocery or dining category cards typically don't
cover wine-club shipments since these purchases usually code as online retail, so a
strong online-shopping or flat-rate cashback card is the most reliable way to earn on
Naked Wines orders.

affiliate.offer: up to 60% off with code IMPACT12PK

stores.name: NAPA Auto Parts
stores.intro:
NAPA Auto Parts is one of the largest independent auto parts networks in North
America, with roughly 6,000 U.S. stores operated by independent jobbers and Genuine
Parts Company. NAPA carries replacement parts, batteries, tools, and chemicals for
DIY drivers and professional repair shops. Auto parts purchases typically code as
general retail rather than triggering a dedicated bonus category.

Cards with selectable cash back categories can sometimes treat NAPA as an auto
category, with Citi Custom Cash and Bank of America Customized Cash both rewarding
the top-spend or selected category. Online orders through napaonline.com may also
qualify for online shopping bonuses on the Chase Freedom Flex when that category
rotates in.

stores.name: Nars
stores.intro:
Nars is a makeup and skincare brand founded by photographer and makeup artist Francois
Nars, known for products like the Orgasm blush and a range that leans into bold color
and a photo-ready finish. Shiseido acquired Nars in 2000, and the brand sells through
its own site, department-store beauty counters, and retailers like Sephora and Ulta.
There is no Nars-branded credit card, so shoppers should look to a department-store or
general online-shopping rewards card, or a flat-rate cashback card, to earn on Nars
purchases rather than expecting a beauty-specific bonus category.

stores.name: National Car Rental
stores.intro:
National is the upmarket Enterprise sister brand, beloved by frequent business
travelers for its Emerald Aisle "pick any car" benefit. Rentals code as car rentals
on every card we track. Several premium cards confer Emerald Club Executive (Amex
Platinum, Capital One Venture X) which adds Executive Aisle access — a wider
selection of higher-tier vehicles. National rarely publishes deep promo codes, so
paying with a 3x-on-travel card often beats hunting for portal discounts.

stores.name: Native Shoes
stores.intro:
Native Shoes is a Vancouver-founded footwear brand best known for lightweight,
injection-molded EVA shoes in the style of the Jefferson sneaker, marketed as an
easy-care, machine-washable alternative to canvas sneakers for kids and adults alike.
It sells primarily direct-to-consumer online alongside placement in outdoor and family
retailers. There is no Native Shoes credit card, so the best approach for these
purchases is a card that rewards select clothing or shoe purchases, or a general
online-shopping or flat-rate cashback card if a category-specific multiplier isn't
available.

stores.name: Nautica
stores.intro:
Nautica is an American apparel brand built around a nautical, preppy aesthetic, selling
men's, women's, and kids' clothing along with accessories and home goods, and it's
licensed and operated today under Authentic Brands Group. Nautica products show up both
at its own stores and site and widely across department stores like Macy's and Kohl's.
There's no Nautica-branded credit card, so shoppers should reach for a department-store
rewards card when buying in-store, or a select-clothing or general online-shopping card
for purchases made directly at nautica.com.

stores.name: NBA League Pass
stores.intro:
NBA League Pass is the league's own direct-to-consumer streaming subscription, giving
fans access to live and on-demand out-of-market NBA games outside of national broadcast
windows. It's sold directly by the NBA rather than bundled through a traditional cable
package, and billed as a recurring subscription much like other streaming services.
There's no NBA-branded credit card tied specifically to League Pass, so a card that
earns bonus rewards on streaming subscriptions, or a general entertainment-category
card, is the best fit for this recurring charge rather than a sports-specific product.

stores.name: Neiman Marcus
stores.intro:
Neiman Marcus is a luxury department store with about 35 full-line stores and a
significant online presence. Average tickets here are among the highest in U.S.
department retail, which shifts the math toward cards that pay a high department-store
category rate without a tight spend cap. NM's own InCircle loyalty program runs
alongside any card cashback you earn at checkout.

stores.name: Netflix
stores.intro:
Netflix is the largest U.S. streaming-video service. Monthly subscriptions code
cleanly as streaming on every card we track. Several mid-tier rewards cards earn
elevated rates on streaming (Blue Cash Preferred 6% on select streaming including
Netflix, U.S. Bank Cash+ 5% if streaming is a chosen category). Card-issued
Netflix credits are rare; the simplest play is pairing the subscription with
whichever streaming-bonus card you already carry.

stores.name: NETGEAR
stores.intro:
NETGEAR is a networking hardware company that makes routers, mesh Wi-Fi systems, modems,
switches, and smart-home security cameras through its Arlo-adjacent and Orbi product
lines, sold directly at netgear.com as well as through electronics retailers like Best
Buy. There's no NETGEAR co-brand credit card, so the smart play is a card that earns
bonus rewards on electronics purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card if the purchase is
a one-off router or Wi-Fi extender rather than part of a recurring spend category.

stores.name: New Balance
stores.intro:
New Balance is a privately held Boston-based athletic footwear and apparel company
with about 100 U.S. brand and factory stores plus a strong DTC channel at
newbalance.com. Direct purchases typically code as department store in person and
online shopping for web orders, while New Balance gear bought at Dick's, Foot Locker,
or running specialty stores codes under that retailer. The free New Balance Rewards
program stacks with credit card multipliers.

stores.name: New Era
stores.intro:
New Era is the headwear company that holds the official on-field cap license for MLB and
supplies sideline and licensed caps for the NFL, NBA, and other leagues, alongside its
own streetwear and lifestyle cap lines. It sells through neweracap.com, team shops, and
general retailers. There is no New Era credit card, so a shopper buying fitted or
snapback caps should lean on a select-clothing or online-shopping rewards card, or a
flat-rate cashback card, rather than expecting a sports-merchandise specific bonus
category.

stores.name: Newegg
stores.intro:
Newegg is an online retailer focused on consumer electronics, PC components, and
gaming gear, with a long history of catering to enthusiast PC builders. The site also
hosts a third-party marketplace for adjacent categories. Newegg codes as online
shopping, so any online-shopping category bonus card (Chase Freedom Flex when
activated, Citi Custom Cash on online shopping, Capital One SavorOne promotions) will
earn its multiplier. Newegg has issued co-brand financing cards in the past (Newegg
Store Card via Comenity, Visa via Synchrony), so check the current offering at
checkout if 0% APR financing is the goal.

stores.name: NFL+
stores.intro:
NFL+ is the NFL's direct-to-consumer streaming subscription, offering live local and
primetime game streams on mobile, NFL Network programming, and on-demand replays and
coaches film for subscribers, sold and billed directly by the league rather than through
a cable provider. There is no NFL+-specific credit card, though league-branded cards
exist tied to some team partnerships. For the subscription charge itself, a card that
earns extra rewards on streaming services, or a solid flat-rate cashback card, is the
practical way to earn on an NFL+ subscription.

stores.name: Nike
stores.intro:
Nike is the world's largest athletic apparel and footwear brand, with about 270
U.S. stores plus a strong digital presence. Nike.com and the Nike app code as
online shopping; in-store charges code as department stores. Apple Card pays
3% on Nike via Apple Pay (in-store and online), one of the few merchant-specific
3% Apple Pay bonuses. Otherwise pair with an online-shopping-bonus card for
digital orders and flat-rate cashback for in-store.

stores.name: Nintendo eShop
stores.intro: The Nintendo eShop is the digital storefront for Switch games, DLC, and add-on content, payable by card or prepaid eShop credit. Purchases code as online shopping or digital entertainment, so a card with an online-purchase bonus earns the most. Buying discounted eShop gift cards from a grocery or warehouse store first can beat charging the eShop directly.

stores.name: Nordstrom
stores.intro:
Nordstrom is a higher-end department store with about 100 full-line stores and a much
larger Nordstrom Rack outlet network. Pricing skews full-retail at the flagship
locations and steeply discounted at Rack, so the right card depends partly on which
channel you're shopping. Both code as department stores at the register and online
shopping on nordstrom.com / nordstromrack.com. Nordstrom's own Nordy Club rewards layer
in on top of card cashback.

stores.name: Nordstrom Rack
stores.intro:
Nordstrom Rack is the off-price arm of Nordstrom, with about 240 stores nationwide
plus the nordstromrack.com site. The format sells the same designer and contemporary
brands as the parent department store at marked-down prices, sourced from previous
season inventory and direct buys. Nordstrom Rack purchases earn Nordy Club points
alongside the main Nordstrom store, and the Nordstrom credit and debit cards
(Visa-branded variants issued by TD Bank) earn elevated points at both Nordstrom and
Nordstrom Rack. Department-store category bonus cards also apply at the register.

stores.name: Northern Tool + Equipment
stores.intro: Northern Tool + Equipment sells generators, air compressors, and trailers, and its stores may code as a home-improvement or hardware merchant or as general retail depending on the location. If your card carries a home-improvement bonus, it can apply, though coding is not guaranteed. Orders at northerntool.com typically code as online shopping, so a flexible flat-rate card hedges nicely.

stores.name: Norwegian Cruise Line
stores.intro: Norwegian Cruise Line is known for its freestyle cruising format with flexible dining and no fixed seating. Fares paid to NCL usually code as travel and reward best on cards with a general travel multiplier, though cruise coding can be uneven. A reliable flat-rate or travel card avoids surprises, since onboard charges may post under a different category.

stores.name: Nutribullet
stores.intro:
Nutribullet is a kitchen appliance brand best known for its compact, high-torque blenders
marketed around single-serve smoothies and nutrient extraction, and it has since
expanded into full-size blenders, food processors, and air fryers. It sells directly at
nutribullet.com as well as through big-box and department retailers. There's no
Nutribullet-branded credit card, so purchases are best covered by a card that earns
bonus rewards on home-goods or kitchenware purchases, or a general online-shopping or
flat-rate cashback card for a one-time appliance buy.

stores.name: O'Reilly Auto Parts
stores.intro:
O'Reilly Auto Parts is the second-largest U.S. auto parts retailer with about
6,200 domestic stores. In-store charges typically code as auto parts; online
orders code as online shopping. The O'Reilly O'Rewards loyalty program is free
and issues per-purchase points redeemable for store credit. Pair with a
flat-rate cashback card or an online-shopping-bonus card for orders shipped
from oreillyauto.com.

stores.name: Off-White
stores.intro:
Off-White is a luxury streetwear label founded by the late Virgil Abloh, known for
diagonal-striped branding, quotation-mark graphics, and high-profile collaborations
spanning apparel, sneakers, and accessories. LVMH holds a majority stake in the brand,
which sells through its own flagship stores and site as well as luxury multi-brand
retailers. There is no Off-White credit card, so purchases are best covered by a
select-clothing or general online-shopping rewards card, or a premium travel or
flat-rate cashback card given the brand's higher price points.

stores.name: Office Depot
stores.intro:
Office Depot (which absorbed OfficeMax) is the second-largest U.S. office-supply
retailer with about 870 stores. In-store and online charges code as office
supplies on every card we track. Chase Ink Business Cash earns 5% back at
Office Depot (capped within the office-supply + telecom $25K/yr bucket) —
typically the strongest pairing for small-business cardholders. The Office
Depot Rewards program adds tier-based points redeemable for store credit.

stores.name: OLAPLEX
stores.intro:
Olaplex is a hair care brand built around bond-building chemistry originally developed
for use in salons, with its No. 3 Hair Perfector becoming a widely recognized at-home
treatment product. The company sells direct at olaplex.com and through beauty retailers
like Sephora and Ulta as well as professional salons. There is no Olaplex-branded credit
card, so shoppers should look to a general online-shopping rewards card or a flat-rate
cashback card to earn on Olaplex purchases, since beauty and hair care don't typically
have their own dedicated bonus category.

stores.name: Old Navy
stores.intro:
Old Navy is Gap, Inc.'s value-priced apparel banner with about 1,200 U.S. stores and a
strong online catalog. Apparel doesn't have a dedicated reward category on most
general-purpose credit cards, so card choice usually comes down to whether you're
shopping online (where online-shopping bonuses apply) or in-store (where you're
typically falling back to a strong flat-rate cashback card).

stores.name: Olipop
stores.intro:
Olipop is a functional soda brand that markets prebiotic-fortified sodas as a healthier
alternative to traditional sugary soft drinks, sold direct at olipop.com by subscription
or one-time order, as well as in grocery and convenience stores nationwide. It's an
independent, venture-backed beverage company with no credit card of its own. For direct
online orders, a general online-shopping or flat-rate cashback card is the practical
choice, while purchases made in a grocery store aisle would instead earn under a
groceries-category card.

stores.name: Olive Garden
stores.intro:
Olive Garden is a U.S. casual-dining chain with about 900 domestic locations,
owned by Darden Restaurants. Charges code as dining on every card we track, so
Amex Gold 4x (capped), Sapphire Preferred 3x, and SavorOne 3% all earn the
dining bonus. Eligible Darden gift cards purchased through the My Olive Garden
Rewards or Darden portals occasionally code as gift cards rather than dining,
worth checking before bulk-buying.

stores.name: Ollie's Bargain Outlet
stores.intro:
Ollie's Bargain Outlet is a closeout retailer with more than 550 stores across the
Eastern and Central U.S., selling brand-name merchandise (books, household goods,
furniture, food, seasonal items) acquired from manufacturer overstock and store
closeouts. The chain's tagline "Good Stuff Cheap" captures the model. Ollie's
typically codes as a department store or discount retailer for credit card networks,
so a department-store category bonus card can earn its multiplier. The free Ollie's
Army loyalty program adds member-only discounts but is not a credit card product.

stores.name: Omaha Steaks
stores.intro:
Omaha Steaks is a family-owned, direct-to-consumer purveyor of steaks, seafood, and other
frozen meats and prepared foods, shipped nationwide from its Nebraska base and popular
both for personal restocking and as a gift item. Orders are placed and shipped directly
through omahasteaks.com rather than through a grocery store, so they often code as
online retail or dining rather than groceries. There's no Omaha Steaks credit card, so a
dining-category card, or a general online-shopping or flat-rate cashback card, is the
best way to earn on an order.

stores.name: Omni Hotels
stores.intro:
Omni Hotels & Resorts is a privately held US luxury and upper-upscale hotel chain with about
50 properties spanning convention-center anchors like the Omni Atlanta and Omni Dallas, resort
destinations like the Omni Grove Park Inn and Omni Bedford Springs, and downtown business
hotels. The Select Guest loyalty program offers free WiFi, beverages, and morning coffee at
every tier. Omni charges code as lodging/hotels. Hotel-bonus cards earn full rate: Chase
Sapphire Reserve at 4x direct, Amex Platinum at 5x prepaid, Capital One Venture X at 2x base.

stores.name: One Kings Lane
stores.intro:
One Kings Lane is an online home decor and furniture retailer that built its name on
flash sales of designer furnishings before shifting to a more traditional e-commerce
storefront covering furniture, lighting, rugs, and decorative accessories. It sells
exclusively online rather than through physical stores. There's no One Kings Lane credit
card, so shoppers furnishing a home through the site should look to a furniture or
home-goods rewards card, or a general online-shopping or flat-rate cashback card for
smaller decor purchases.

stores.name: OneTravel
stores.intro:
OneTravel is an online travel agency that lets shoppers search and book flights, hotels,
car rentals, and vacation packages across multiple airlines and hotel chains from a
single site, similar to other discount OTAs. Because it's a booking aggregator rather
than a single airline or hotel brand, purchases typically post under a general travel
merchant category. There's no OneTravel credit card, so a broad travel-rewards card that
earns bonus points on travel-category purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the
best way to earn when booking through OneTravel.

stores.name: Orbitz
stores.intro: Orbitz is an online travel agency in the Expedia Group family, bundling flights, hotels, and packages with its Orbucks rewards. Bookings usually code as travel and earn on cards with a travel multiplier, though individual components may sometimes code under the supplier. A flexible travel card is the strongest fit for both standalone and packaged reservations here.

stores.name: Osprey
stores.intro:
Osprey is an outdoor gear company known for its backpacking, hiking, and travel packs,
built around features like its All Mighty Guarantee lifetime repair policy that has made
it a favorite among thru-hikers and backpackers. Products are sold direct at osprey.com
as well as through outdoor retailers like REI. There's no Osprey credit card, so a
sporting-goods or outdoor-retailer rewards card is the natural fit for a pack purchase,
with a general online-shopping or flat-rate cashback card as a solid alternative.

stores.name: Outback Steakhouse
stores.intro:
Outback Steakhouse is a U.S. casual-dining steakhouse chain with about 700
domestic locations, owned by Bloomin' Brands (also Carrabba's, Bonefish Grill).
Charges code as dining on every card we track. Any dining-bonus card applies
(Amex Gold 4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%). The Dine Rewards program
spans Bloomin' Brands sister concepts and stacks on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Outdoor Voices
stores.intro:
Outdoor Voices is an activewear brand built around a "doing things" ethos that pitches
itself as more casual and everyday than performance-focused athletic brands, known for
leggings, the Exercise Dress, and its colorful, minimalist aesthetic. It sells primarily
direct-to-consumer through outdoorvoices.com and a small number of its own retail
locations. There is no Outdoor Voices credit card, so a select-clothing or
sporting-goods rewards card is a good fit for activewear purchases, with a general
online-shopping or flat-rate cashback card as a fallback.

stores.name: Overstock
stores.intro: Overstock, now operating as Bed Bath & Beyond online, sells furniture, rugs, and home goods at outlet prices. Orders code as online shopping rather than as a home-improvement category, so a flat-rate or online-retail card earns the most. Its larger furniture buys make a strong online-shopping multiplier especially worthwhile.

stores.name: Owlet
stores.intro:
Owlet is a baby-tech company best known for its Smart Sock and camera products that
monitor an infant's heart rate, oxygen levels, and sleep, aimed at giving new parents
peace of mind overnight. It sells direct at owletcare.com and through major baby and
electronics retailers. There is no Owlet-branded credit card, so a purchase is best
covered by a card that rewards electronics or online-shopping purchases, or a
flat-rate cashback card, since baby-monitoring tech doesn't have its own dedicated
bonus category.

stores.name: OXO
stores.intro:
OXO is a kitchen and household tools brand best known for its Good Grips line of
ergonomic, easy-to-use utensils, gadgets, and storage products, now owned by Newell
Brands alongside names like Rubbermaid and Sharpie. Products are sold at oxo.com and
widely across grocery, home-goods, and department stores. There's no OXO-branded credit
card, so a card that earns bonus rewards on home-improvement or kitchenware purchases is
the best fit, with a general online-shopping or flat-rate cashback card as a solid
fallback for smaller orders.

stores.name: P.F. Chang's
stores.intro:
P.F. Chang's China Bistro operates roughly 200 US upscale-casual restaurants known for the
Chang's Spicy Chicken, Mongolian Beef, Dynamite Shrimp, and signature lettuce wraps in a
pan-Asian table-service format. Most locations sit in upscale shopping centers or near
high-end malls. P.F. Chang's transactions code as dining/restaurants, including delivery and
takeout orders. Cards offering elevated dining bonuses (Amex Gold at 4x Membership Rewards,
Chase Sapphire Reserve at 4x Ultimate Rewards, Capital One Savor at 3% cash) earn full rate.

stores.name: Pacsun
stores.intro:
Pacsun is a California-rooted apparel chain catering to teens and young adults, with
about 325 mall-based stores and a strong online business at pacsun.com. Most cards
code in-store transactions as department store and web orders as online shopping. The
Pacsun Rewards program is free and points-based, layering on top of credit card
earnings since the chain does not run a branded credit card in the U.S.

stores.name: Panda Express
stores.intro: Panda Express is the largest American Chinese fast-food chain, best known for its Orange Chicken and mall-and-highway locations nationwide. Purchases code as dining or restaurants on most rewards cards, so a dining multiplier captures the value. Combo orders at a food court counter still register under the same restaurant code as standalone stores.

stores.name: Pandora
stores.intro:
Pandora is a Danish jewelry company best known for its customizable charm bracelets
plus rings, earrings, and lab-grown diamond collections. The U.S. footprint covers
about 360 concept stores and shop-in-shops plus us.pandora.net. Direct purchases
typically code as department store in person and online shopping for web orders. The
free Pandora Club loyalty program offers points and a birthday gift, but Pandora does
not run a U.S. branded credit card.

stores.name: Panera Bread
stores.intro:
Panera Bread is a fast-casual bakery-cafe with about 2,100 U.S. locations. Charges
code as dining on every card we track. The Unlimited Sip Club ($14.99/mo) provides
unlimited drinks and is occasionally credited by the Capital One SavorOne or Amex
Gold's flexible dining benefits. Standard high-bonus dining cards (Amex Gold 4x,
Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%) are the obvious pairings.

stores.name: Papa John's
stores.intro:
Papa John's is a U.S. pizza-delivery chain with about 3,300 domestic locations.
Charges code as dining on every card we track. Any dining-bonus card (Amex Gold
4x, Sapphire Preferred 3x, SavorOne 3%) applies. The Papa Rewards loyalty
program in the app issues recurring points-for-pizza promotions that stack on
top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Papa Murphy's
stores.intro: Papa Murphy's sells take-and-bake pizzas you finish in your own oven, the largest chain of its kind. Despite the grocery-like format, transactions typically code as dining or restaurants rather than supermarket, so a card with a restaurant bonus usually beats a grocery card here.

stores.name: Paramount+
stores.intro: Paramount+ streams CBS, Paramount, and Showtime content along with live sports like NFL and soccer. The subscription codes as streaming, so a card with a streaming bonus rewards it best, while other cards earn flat-rate. Choosing the annual plan over monthly and charging it to a streaming-category card maximizes both the discount and the points.

stores.name: Patagonia
stores.intro:
Patagonia is a privately held outdoor and performance apparel brand owned by the
Holdfast Collective, with about 40 U.S. retail stores plus a strong DTC business at
patagonia.com. Direct purchases typically code as department store in person and
online shopping for web orders, while the Worn Wear resale platform issues store
credit for trade-ins. Patagonia does not offer a U.S. branded credit card, so most
shoppers rely on a strong online or everyday multiplier for full-price gear.

stores.name: Paul Smith
stores.intro:
Paul Smith is a British designer fashion house founded by its namesake, known for
tailored menswear with a signature multicolor stripe motif alongside womenswear,
accessories, and eyewear. It remains one of the few major British fashion labels still
largely independent, selling through its own boutiques and site as well as select luxury
department stores. There's no Paul Smith credit card, so shoppers should reach for a
select-clothing or general online-shopping rewards card, or a premium travel or
flat-rate cashback card given the brand's higher-end pricing.

stores.name: Pavilions
stores.intro:
Pavilions is the upscale Albertsons banner serving affluent Southern California
neighborhoods, with a focus on prepared foods, wine, and specialty grocery. It codes as
a U.S. supermarket, so any grocery category bonus (Amex Gold, Blue Cash Preferred, Citi
Custom Cash) applies at the register. Like other Albertsons banners, Pavilions runs the
Albertsons for U loyalty program, and the broad gift-card rack at the service counter
is worth knowing about if you want to extend a 4x or 6x grocery multiplier into other
spending.

stores.name: Peacock
stores.intro: Peacock is NBCUniversal's streaming service, carrying network shows, movies, and live events including Premier League and Olympics coverage. Monthly charges code as streaming, so a card with a streaming category bonus is the strongest fit. If you prefer the ad-supported tier, the lower price still earns the same streaming bonus rate on the right card.

stores.name: Peak Design
stores.intro:
Peak Design is a San Francisco based maker of camera bags, straps, tripods, and travel
gear, best known for launching many of its products through record-setting Kickstarter
campaigns before selling direct at peakdesign.com. The company builds its own carry
systems, like the Capture clip and modular Travel Backpack line, aimed at photographers
and frequent travelers who want gear that doubles as everyday carry. There is no Peak
Design co-brand credit card, so shoppers are best served by a flat-rate cashback card or
a card that boosts online shopping purchases when checking out directly on the brand's
site.

stores.name: Pearle Vision
stores.intro: Pearle Vision is an EssilorLuxottica optical chain built around neighborhood eye-care centers offering exams, frames, and contacts. Online orders code as online shopping and reward a card with an online or general retail bonus, while in-store charges usually settle at flat-rate. Since vision plans and FSA dollars often cover part of the bill, factor that in before optimizing for points.

stores.name: Peet's Coffee
stores.intro:
Peet's Coffee is a specialty coffee roaster and cafe chain with about 200 U.S.
cafes, primarily in California. In-cafe charges code as dining on every card we
track. Bagged-bean orders shipped from peets.com sometimes code as online shopping
rather than dining — worth checking before relying on a 4x dining card. Heavy
regulars stack the Peetnik Rewards loyalty program on top of card bonuses.

stores.name: Penske Truck Rental
stores.intro:
Penske Truck Rental is one of the largest consumer and commercial truck rental
operations in North America, renting box trucks, cargo vans, and pickups for moves,
hauling, and small business use out of thousands of locations. It operates alongside
Penske Truck Leasing and Penske Logistics under the broader Penske Corporation,
founded by racing executive Roger Penske. There is no Penske co-brand credit card, so
a general rental car and travel category card, or a flat-rate cashback card, is the
practical choice for earning rewards on a rental booked through Penske.

stores.name: Pep Boys
stores.intro:
Pep Boys operates roughly 800 auto parts and service centers across the United
States, combining a parts counter with tire installation, oil changes, brake work,
and full-service mechanical repair. Most credit cards code Pep Boys purchases as
auto services or general retail rather than as a dedicated bonus category, so
flat-rate cards like the Citi Double Cash often deliver the cleanest 2 percent.

Cards with selectable categories can reach Pep Boys when the right bucket is
chosen. Bank of America Customized Cash and U.S. Bank Cash+ both have wider
categories that may capture auto service spending, and Citi Custom Cash pays 5
percent on the top eligible spending category for cardholders whose monthly spend
skews toward vehicle maintenance.

stores.name: Perricone MD
stores.intro:
Perricone MD is a skincare brand founded by dermatologist Dr. Nicholas Perricone,
known for anti-aging formulas built around ingredients like vitamin C ester and
neuropeptides, sold direct at perriconemd.com as well as through department stores and
specialty beauty retailers. The line spans serums, moisturizers, and supplements
positioned at the premium end of the skincare market. There is no Perricone MD
co-brand credit card, so a card that earns bonus rewards on online shopping or
department store purchases, or a general beauty and drugstore category card, is the
better route for maximizing rewards on a purchase.

stores.name: Personalization Mall
stores.intro:
Personalization Mall sells custom-engraved and monogrammed gifts, including
ornaments, home decor, drinkware, and kids' items, that shoppers personalize with
names, dates, or photos before checkout. The company was acquired by 1-800-Flowers.com
in 2018 and now operates as part of that company's family of gifting brands alongside
1-800-Flowers and Harry & David. There is no Personalization Mall co-brand credit
card, so a card that rewards online shopping purchases, or a flat-rate cashback card,
is the simplest way to earn something back on a personalized gift order.

stores.name: Pet Supermarket
stores.intro:
Pet Supermarket is a Southeast U.S. pet specialty chain with around 200 stores
across Florida, Georgia, the Carolinas, Alabama, and neighboring states. The
retailer carries dog and cat food, small animal supplies, fish, reptiles, and live
bird departments. Most Pet Supermarket locations code as pet shops or general
retail on credit card networks, not as a standalone rewards category.

Cards with selectable categories like U.S. Bank Cash+ can include pet shops in
their lineup, while flat-rate cards such as the Citi Double Cash or Wells Fargo
Active Cash deliver a steady 2 percent on every pet food run. Online orders
through petsupermarket.com may trigger online shopping bonuses on Bank of America
Customized Cash and Chase Freedom Flex rotating quarters.

stores.name: Pet Supplies Plus
stores.intro:
Pet Supplies Plus is the third-largest pet specialty chain in the United States
with over 700 neighborhood stores carrying food, treats, toys, and grooming
services for dogs, cats, fish, reptiles, and small animals. Most Pet Supplies Plus
locations code as pet shops or general retail on credit card networks, not as a
category that triggers standard rewards bonuses.

Cards with selectable categories sometimes include pet shops, with U.S. Bank Cash+
offering pet-related categories in its lineup. Online orders placed through
petsuppliesplus.com may also trigger online shopping bonuses on cards like Bank of
America Customized Cash and Chase Freedom Flex. The free Preferred Pet Club
loyalty program adds points on top of credit card rewards.

stores.name: Pet Valu
stores.intro: Pet Valu is a pet-supply retailer selling food, treats, and accessories, with much of its US rewards value coming from shopping online. Site purchases generally code as online shopping or general retail, earning on cards with an online or everyday-purchase bonus, while in-store buys usually fall to flat-rate. A solid flat-rate card covers recurring pet runs reliably.

stores.name: Petco
stores.intro:
Petco is one of the two large U.S. pet specialty retailers (alongside PetSmart),
with about 1,500 stores plus in-store grooming and veterinary services. In-store
charges code as pet store / specialty retail on most cards (some classify as
groceries for food-only baskets); petco.com codes as online shopping. The Vital
Care Premier membership ($24.99/mo) credits back monthly spend at Petco. Pair
with a flat-rate cashback or online-shopping-bonus card for petco.com orders.

stores.name: Peter Thomas Roth
stores.intro:
Peter Thomas Roth Labs is a New York founded skincare brand known for clinical-style
formulas such as its Water Drench hyaluronic acid line and Instant FIRMx treatments,
sold direct online as well as through Sephora, Ulta, and department stores. The brand
markets itself on dermatologist-inspired ingredients and frequent product innovation
rather than a single hero item. There is no Peter Thomas Roth co-brand credit card, so
shoppers should lean on an online shopping or department store rewards card, or a
general beauty and drugstore category card, when purchasing from the brand.

stores.name: PetSmart
stores.intro:
PetSmart is the largest U.S. pet specialty retailer by store count at about 1,650
locations, with a sizable petsmart.com online catalog. Pet-supply purchases don't have a
dedicated category on most credit cards, so in-store purchases generally fall back to a
flat-rate cashback card. Online purchases at petsmart.com code as online shopping. The
Treats Rewards loyalty program stacks on top of card cashback.

stores.name: Philips Hue
stores.intro:
Philips Hue is a smart home lighting system offering color-changing and tunable-white
bulbs, light strips, and fixtures that connect over Zigbee and a bridge hub to work with
Apple HomeKit, Amazon Alexa, and Google Home. The Philips Hue brand is made and sold
under license by Signify, the lighting company spun out of the former Philips Lighting
business. There is no Philips Hue co-brand credit card, so a card that earns bonus
rewards at electronics stores, or a card boosting online shopping purchases when buying
direct from philips-hue.com, is the better fit for smart lighting purchases.

stores.name: Phillips 66
stores.intro:
Phillips 66 is a U.S. refiner whose retail footprint covers more than 7,000 branded
gas stations across three names: Phillips 66 in the central U.S., 76 on the West
Coast, and Conoco in the Mountain West. All three brands code as gas at the pump, so
any gas category bonus card (Costco Anywhere Visa, Citi Custom Cash on gas, Sam's
Club Mastercard) will earn its multiplier. The KickBack Rewards loyalty app offers
per-gallon discounts that stack with rewards card earn at participating sites.

stores.name: Philo
stores.intro:
Philo is a low-cost live TV streaming service focused on entertainment, lifestyle,
and reality programming from networks like AMC, Discovery, A&E, and Comedy Central,
deliberately skipping pricier sports and local broadcast channels to keep its monthly
price well below competitors like Hulu with Live TV or YouTube TV. The service is
subscription only, with no cable box or contract required. There is no Philo co-brand
credit card, so a streaming or entertainment category card, or a flat-rate cashback
card, is the practical way to earn rewards on a Philo subscription charge.

stores.name: Philz Coffee
stores.intro:
Philz Coffee is a San Francisco-born specialty chain of about 70 cafes across California,
the East Coast, and the DC region, built around custom pour-over brews like Tesora, Ether,
and the Mint Mojito iced coffee. Orders placed in-cafe, in the Philz app, or ahead via
curbside all code as dining/restaurants. Top dining cards earn their full rate at Philz:
Amex Gold pays 4x Membership Rewards, Chase Sapphire Reserve pays 4x Ultimate Rewards,
and Capital One SavorOne pays 3% unlimited cash back on every order.

stores.name: Pick 'n Save
stores.intro: Pick 'n Save is the largest supermarket chain in Wisconsin and operates under the Kroger umbrella. Your cart total codes as groceries on most rewards cards, so a card with a supermarket multiplier earns the most. If you fill up at an attached Pick 'n Save fuel center, that swipe codes as a gas purchase instead.

stores.name: Pilot Flying J
stores.intro:
Pilot Flying J operates more than 750 travel centers across the U.S. and Canada,
making it the largest truck stop network in North America. The chain serves both
long-haul truckers (with high-flow diesel lanes, showers, and driver lounges) and
passenger drivers at standard gas pumps. Fuel at Pilot Flying J pumps codes as gas, so
any card with a gas category bonus will earn its rate. The myRewards Plus loyalty
program adds per-gallon discounts and points on in-store food, which can stack with a
separate gas rewards card.

stores.name: Pizza Hut
stores.intro:
